# DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

![UI Lib](https://daqem.com/img/projects/ui-lib.webp)

UI Lib
======

A user interface library for Minecraft modding.

[](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/ui)
[](https://modrinth.com/mod/ui-lib)
[](https://github.com/DAQEM/UILib)

**UI Lib** is a comprehensive user interface library designed for Minecraft mod developers. It abstracts away the complexities of the raw Minecraft `Screen` and `GuiGraphics` classes, providing a robust component-based system for building modern, responsive, and interactive GUIs.

Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#features "Direct link to Features")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 🧩 Component System

Build GUIs using a hierarchical system of Components and Widgets. Easily nest elements, handle positioning relative to parents, and manage rendering layers.

### 🎨 Rich Visuals

Includes built-in support for gradients, blurred backgrounds, panoramas, item rendering, and sprite manipulation out of the box.

### 📜 Advanced Text

Forget standard strings. Use Multi-line text, scrolling marquees, and truncated text components that automatically handle width constraints.

### 🌳 Skill Tree Engine

A complete engine for rendering draggable, zoomable skill trees with automatic node positioning algorithms.

For Users[​](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#for-users "Direct link to For Users")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If a mod you are using requires **UI Lib**, simply download the version matching your Minecraft version and Mod Loader and place it in your `mods` folder.

For Developers[​](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#for-developers "Direct link to For Developers")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

UI Lib handles the heavy lifting of coordinate calculations, scissor testing (clipping), and input handling.

Check the **[Getting Started](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib/wiki/getting-started)
** guide to integrate it into your project.

*   [Features](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#features)
    
*   [For Users](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#for-users)
    
*   [For Developers](https://daqem.com/projects/ui-lib#for-developers)

# DAQEM Studios is officially a Hytale Partner! | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

This is perhaps the biggest announcement in the history of DAQEM Studios. I am incredibly proud to finally reveal that **DAQEM Studios has officially become a Hytale Partner.**

For years, you have known me as a solo developer crafting detailed mods for the Minecraft ecosystem. Today, we take the next massive step forward.

### A New Era Begins[​](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#a-new-era-begins "Direct link to A New Era Begins")

Hytale has been on the horizon for a long time, promising a sandbox experience built from the ground up with creators and modders in mind. The level of depth they are offering to developers is unprecedented in this genre.

Becoming a Hytale Partner is a tremendous honor. It means Hypixel Studios recognizes the work I have put into modding over the years and trusts me to bring that same quality to their new universe. This partnership grants me the resources and early insight needed to hit the ground running.

### The Hytale Beta and DAQEM Mods[​](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#the-hytale-beta-and-daqem-mods "Direct link to The Hytale Beta and DAQEM Mods")

As many of you know, the highly anticipated Hytale Beta begins on **January 13th**.

I will be diving into the Hytale Model Maker and their extensive scripting tools the moment the doors open.

**So, what mods am I making?**

To be honest: I don't know yet! And that’s the most exciting part. Hytale is a completely new canvas. I want to get my hands on the tools, explore the base game, and see what the community needs and what inspires me. Whether it's quality-of-life improvements, complex gameplay mechanics, or decorative expansions, you can expect the same level of polish you’ve come to expect from my Minecraft projects.

### Support the Studio[​](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#support-the-studio "Direct link to Support the Studio")

DAQEM Studios has always been, and remains, just me. One person passionate about creating great experiences for players.

If you are planning to join the Beta and haven't bought the game yet, you can support me directly. Please consider using my Creator Code when **purchasing your copy of Hytale**:

"DAQEM"

Using this code at checkout supports me directly and allows me to dedicate more time to learning Hytale's engine and developing complex, high-quality mods for everyone to enjoy.

Thank you all for your continued support over the years. The Minecraft journey has been incredible, but the Hytale adventure is just beginning. See you in Orbis on January 13th!

  

> P.S. Of course, I will continue to support my existing Minecraft mods for as long as possible. DAQEM Studios is not going anywhere!

*   [A New Era Begins](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#a-new-era-begins)
    
*   [The Hytale Beta and DAQEM Mods](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#the-hytale-beta-and-daqem-mods)
    
*   [Support the Studio](https://daqem.com/news/hytale-partnership-announcement#support-the-studio)

# DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

**Privacy Policy**

**Last updated:** November 14, 2025

This Privacy Policy outlines our procedures regarding the collection, use, and disclosure of your information when you use our website. It also informs you of your privacy rights and how you are protected by law.

By using our Service, you consent to the collection and use of information as described in this Privacy Policy. This policy is designed to comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for our users in the European Union.

### **Key Definitions**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#key-definitions "Direct link to key-definitions")

*   **Company:** Refers to DAQEM Studios, also addressed as "We," "Us," or "Our." For GDPR purposes, the Company is the Data Controller.
*   **Cookies:** Small files placed on your device by a website that store details of your browsing history.
*   **Country:** The Netherlands.
*   **Device:** Any device that can access our Service, such as a computer, cellphone, or tablet.
*   **Personal Data:** Any information that relates to an identified or identifiable individual.
*   **Service:** Refers to the DAQEM Studios website, accessible at [https://daqem.com](https://daqem.com/)
    .
*   **Usage Data:** Data that is collected automatically, either generated by using the Service or from the Service's infrastructure itself (e.g., the duration of a page visit).
*   **You:** The individual or entity using our Service.

### **Data We Collect**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#data-we-collect "Direct link to data-we-collect")

#### **Usage Data**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#usage-data "Direct link to usage-data")

We automatically collect Usage Data when you interact with our Service. This may include:

*   Your Device's Internet Protocol (IP) address
*   Browser type and version
*   The pages of our Service that you visit
*   The time and date of your visit
*   The time spent on those pages
*   Unique device identifiers
*   Other diagnostic data

When you access our Service via a mobile device, we may also collect information such as the type of mobile device, its unique ID, its IP address, your mobile operating system, and the mobile internet browser you use.

#### **Tracking Technologies and Cookies**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#tracking-technologies-and-cookies "Direct link to tracking-technologies-and-cookies")

We use Cookies and similar tracking technologies to monitor activity on our Service and store certain information. The technologies we utilize include:

*   **Cookies or Browser Cookies:** You can set your browser to refuse all Cookies or to be notified when a Cookie is being sent. However, some parts of our Service may not function properly without them.
*   **Web Beacons:** These are small electronic files (also known as clear gifs, pixel tags, and single-pixel gifs) in our website's sections and emails that help us count users who have visited those pages or opened an email.

We use both "Session" and "Persistent" Cookies. Session Cookies are deleted when you close your web browser, while Persistent Cookies remain on your device.

The primary purposes of the cookies we use are:

*   **Analytics Cookies (Third-Party):** We use Google Analytics to track and analyze website traffic. This information helps us understand how users interact with our website and allows us to improve it. Google may use the data collected to personalize ads on its own advertising network.
    *   For more on Google's privacy practices, visit: [https://policies.google.com/privacy](https://policies.google.com/privacy)
        
    *   To opt out of Google Analytics, visit: [https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout](https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout)
        
*   **Advertising Cookies (Third-Party):** We plan to use Google AdSense to display advertisements. Third-party vendors, including Google, use these cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to our website or other websites. This enables Google and its partners to provide you with personalized advertising.
    *   You can opt out of personalized advertising by visiting: [https://www.google.com/settings/ads](https://www.google.com/settings/ads)
         or [http://www.aboutads.info/choices/](http://www.aboutads.info/choices/)
        

### **How We Use Your Personal Data**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#how-we-use-your-personal-data "Direct link to how-we-use-your-personal-data")

We use your Personal Data for the following purposes:

*   **To provide and maintain our Service:** This includes monitoring the usage of our Service.
*   **For data analysis:** We use the data to identify usage trends and to evaluate and enhance our Service, marketing, and your overall experience.
*   **To serve advertisements:** We may use Service Providers to display advertisements to support and maintain our Service.

### **Sharing Your Personal Information**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#sharing-your-personal-information "Direct link to sharing-your-personal-information")

We may share your personal information with the following Service Providers:

*   **Google Analytics:** To monitor and analyze the use of our Service.
*   **Google AdSense:** To display advertisements.

These third-party vendors collect, store, and use your data according to their own Privacy Policies.

### **Transfer and Retention of Your Personal Data**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#transfer-and-retention-of-your-personal-data "Direct link to transfer-and-retention-of-your-personal-data")

Your information, including Personal Data, may be transferred to and maintained on computers located outside of your jurisdiction where data protection laws may differ. Your consent to this Privacy Policy represents your agreement to that transfer.

We will take all reasonable steps to ensure your data is treated securely and in accordance with this Privacy Policy. We will retain your Usage Data for internal analysis, generally for a shorter period, unless it is needed to enhance security, improve our Service's functionality, or we are legally required to retain it for a longer duration.

### **Disclosure of Your Personal Data**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#disclosure-of-your-personal-data "Direct link to disclosure-of-your-personal-data")

We may be required to disclose your Personal Data by law or in response to valid requests from public authorities (e.g., a court or government agency). We may also disclose your data in the good faith belief that it is necessary to:

*   Comply with a legal obligation
*   Protect and defend our rights or property
*   Prevent or investigate potential wrongdoing in connection with the Service
*   Protect the personal safety of our users or the public
*   Protect against legal liability

### **Security of Your Personal Data**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#security-of-your-personal-data "Direct link to security-of-your-personal-data")

The security of your Personal Data is important to us. However, please remember that no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure. While we strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect your data, we cannot guarantee its absolute security.

### **Your Rights Under the GDPR**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#your-rights-under-the-gdpr "Direct link to your-rights-under-the-gdpr")

If you are within the European Union, you have the following rights under this Privacy Policy and by law:

*   **Request access** to the Personal Data we hold about you.
*   **Request correction** of any incomplete or inaccurate information.
*   **Object to the processing** of your Personal Data.
*   **Request erasure** of your Personal Data.
*   **Request the transfer** of your Personal Data.
*   **Withdraw your consent** to the use of your Personal Data.

To exercise these rights, please contact us. We may ask you to verify your identity before responding to such requests. You also have the right to complain to a Data Protection Authority about our collection and use of your Personal Data.

### **Children's Privacy**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#childrens-privacy "Direct link to childrens-privacy")

Our Service is not intended for anyone under the age of 13, and we do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from children under 13. If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided us with Personal Data, please contact us.

### **Links to Other Websites**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#links-to-other-websites "Direct link to links-to-other-websites")

Our Service may contain links to other websites not operated by us. If you click on a third-party link, you will be directed to that site. We strongly advise you to review the Privacy Policy of every site you visit.

### **Changes to This Privacy Policy**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#changes-to-this-privacy-policy "Direct link to changes-to-this-privacy-policy")

We may update our Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new policy on this page and updating the "Last updated" date. You are advised to review this Privacy Policy periodically for any changes.

### **Contact Us**[​](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#contact-us "Direct link to contact-us")

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, you can contact us at:

*   **Email:** admin\[at\]daqem\[dot\]com

*   [**Key Definitions**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#key-definitions)
    
*   [**Data We Collect**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#data-we-collect)
    *   [**Usage Data**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#usage-data)
        
    *   [**Tracking Technologies and Cookies**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#tracking-technologies-and-cookies)
        
*   [**How We Use Your Personal Data**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#how-we-use-your-personal-data)
    
*   [**Sharing Your Personal Information**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#sharing-your-personal-information)
    
*   [**Transfer and Retention of Your Personal Data**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#transfer-and-retention-of-your-personal-data)
    
*   [**Disclosure of Your Personal Data**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#disclosure-of-your-personal-data)
    
*   [**Security of Your Personal Data**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#security-of-your-personal-data)
    
*   [**Your Rights Under the GDPR**](https://daqem.com/privacy-policy#your-rights-under-the-gdpr)

# Installation - Item Restrictions | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/installation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Item Restrictions is available for **Forge**, **Fabric**, and **NeoForge**.

Requirements[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure you have the following installed for your specific loader and Minecraft version:

1.  **Item Restrictions** mod jar.
2.  **Arc Lib** (Required Dependency).
3.  **YAML Config** (Required Dependency).
4.  **Architectury API** (Required Dependency).
5.  **Fabric API** (If using Fabric).

Setup[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/installation#setup "Direct link to Setup")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Download the mods from [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/item-restrictions)
     or [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/item-restrictions)
    .
2.  Place all jar files into your `mods` folder.
3.  Launch the game.

Upon a successful launch, the mod will generate a configuration folder at `config/itemrestrictions/`.

*   [Requirements](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements)
    
*   [Setup](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/installation#setup)

# File Structure - Item Restrictions | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/file-structure#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Item Restrictions uses JSON files to define rules. These files can be placed in two locations:

1.  **Global Config:** `config/itemrestrictions/restrictions/<namespace>/<filename>.json` (Recommended for modpack devs and server admins).
2.  **Datapacks:** `data/<namespace>/itemrestrictions/restrictions/<filename>.json` (Recommended for datapack devs).

The JSON Format[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/file-structure#the-json-format "Direct link to The JSON Format")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here is the anatomy of a restriction file:

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:barrier"    },    "client_side": true,    "types": [        "cant_craft",        "cant_use_item"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:diamond_sword"            }        }    ]}

Fields Explained[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/file-structure#fields-explained "Direct link to Fields Explained")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | Object | No  | Defines an item to represent this restriction (mostly used for internal referencing or future UI). |
| `client_side` | Boolean | No  | Default: `true`. If true, the restriction is checked on the client side to prevent ghost actions. |
| `types` | Array<String> | **Yes** | A list of actions to block. See [Restriction Types](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types)<br>. |
| `conditions` | Array<Object> | **Yes** | A list of **Arc Lib** conditions that must be met for the restriction to apply. See [Conditions](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types)<br>. |

info

**How Logic Works** If **ALL** conditions in the `conditions` list are met, the actions defined in `types` will be **BLOCKED**.

Think of it as: "If \[Conditions\] are true, then \[Restrict Actions\]."

*   [The JSON Format](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/file-structure#the-json-format)
    
*   [Fields Explained](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/file-structure#fields-explained)

---

# SQLite - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

SQLite is the default database system used by GriefLogger. It is a self-contained, serverless, zero-configuration database engine, making it incredibly easy to use.

### How It Works[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

You don't need to do anything to enable SQLite—it's the default setting. When you first start your server with GriefLogger installed, the mod will automatically create a file named `database.db` inside the `config/grieflogger/` directory.

This single file contains all of your server's log data, including every table for blocks, items, sessions, and users.

### Advantages[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#advantages "Direct link to Advantages")

*   **Simplicity:** No external database server to install, configure, or maintain. It just works.
*   **Portability:** Your entire log history is contained in one file. Backing up or moving your server is as simple as copying the `database.db` file along with the rest of your server files.

### Disadvantages[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#disadvantages "Direct link to Disadvantages")

*   **Performance at Scale:** While very efficient, SQLite can become a bottleneck on extremely large servers with hundreds of concurrent players and a massive amount of logged interactions. For most servers, this is not an issue, but if you run a large public network, you should consider using **[MySQL](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql)
    **.

### Management[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#management "Direct link to Management")

#### Backing Up[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#backing-up "Direct link to Backing Up")

To back up your GriefLogger data, simply **shut down your server** and make a copy of the `database.db` file.

Important

Always stop your server before copying or modifying the `database.db` file to prevent data corruption.

#### Resetting Data[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#resetting-data "Direct link to Resetting Data")

To completely wipe all log data, stop your server and delete the `database.db` file. A new, empty database file will be generated the next time the server starts.

*   [How It Works](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#how-it-works)
    
*   [Advantages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#advantages)
    
*   [Disadvantages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#disadvantages)
    
*   [Management](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#management)
    *   [Backing Up](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#backing-up)
        
    *   [Resetting Data](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite#resetting-data)

# Cancel Action - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/cancel_action#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:cancel_action` reward type prevents the default outcome of a triggered action.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/cancel_action#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This reward type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/cancel_action#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example prevents the player from breaking diamond ore.

data/my\_datapack/arc/prevent\_breaking\_diamond\_ore.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:block",            "block": "minecraft:diamond_ore"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:cancel_action"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/cancel_action#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[⛏️ On Break Block\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onbreakblock action type is triggered when a player breaks a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_break_block)

[🐑 On Breed Animal\
------------------\
\
The arc:onbreedanimal action type is triggered when a player breeds two animals.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_breed_animal)

[🥤 On Drink\
-----------\
\
The arc:on\_drink action type is triggered when a player drinks an item (e.g., potion, milk bucket, etc.).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_drink)

[🍽️ On Eat\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_eat action type is triggered when a player eats an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_eat)

[💉 On Effect Added\
------------------\
\
The arc:onaddeffect action type is triggered when a player receives a potion effect.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_add_effect)

[🪣 On Empty Bucket\
------------------\
\
The arc:onemptybucket action type is triggered when a player places a liquid or fish from a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_empty_bucket)

[⚔️ On Get Attack Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:getattackspeed action type is used to retrieve the attack speed of a player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_attack_speed)

[⚡ On Get Destroy Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:ongetdestroy\_speed action type is triggered when a player is determining how quickly they can break a block. This action type allows you to modify the destroy speed based on various conditions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_get_destroy_speed)

[🤕 On Get Hurt\
--------------\
\
The arc:ongethurt action type is triggered when a player takes damage.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_hurt)

[🌾 On Harvest Crop\
------------------\
\
The arc:onharvestcrop action type is triggered when a player harvests a crop.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_harvest_crop)

[🗡️ On Hurt Entity\
------------------\
\
The arc:onhurtentity action type is triggered when a player hurts an entity.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_hurt_entity)

[🗡️ On Hurt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onhurtitem action type is triggered when a player hurts/damages an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_hurt_item)

[🩸 On Hurt Player\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onhurtplayer action type is triggered when a player hurts another player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_hurt_player)

[🖱️ On Interact Block\
---------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractblock action type is triggered when a player interacts with a block, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_interact_block)

[🖱️ On Interact Entity\
----------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractentity action type is triggered when a player interacts with an entity, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_interact_entity)

[⬇️ On Land\

# Pages - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

When you perform a query with `/gl inspect` or `/gl lookup` that returns more than a few results, GriefLogger automatically organizes the output into **pages**. This keeps your chat from being flooded and allows you to navigate the results systematically.

### The Page Footer[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#the-page-footer "Direct link to The Page Footer")

At the end of each page of results, you will see a footer that looks like this:

`⯇ Page 1/5 ⯈`

*   **`⯇` (Left Arrow):** Navigates to the previous page.
*   **`Page 1/5`:** Shows your current page number and the total number of pages.
*   **`⯈` (Right Arrow):** Navigates to the next page.

Clickable Navigation

The arrows in the footer are interactive! Clicking on them in your chat window will automatically execute the correct `/gl page` command to take you to the next or previous page. This is the easiest way to navigate results.

### Page Command[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#page-command "Direct link to Page Command")

If you prefer, you can also navigate manually using the `page` command.

    /grieflogger page <number>

**Alias:** `/gl page`

*   **Permission:** Requires permission level 2 (standard for most server operators).
*   **Argument:** The page number you wish to view.

**Example:** To jump directly to the third page of your last lookup results, you would type:

    /gl page 3

### Session-Specific Results[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#session-specific-results "Direct link to Session-Specific Results")

Your lookup results are tied to your player session. This means two administrators can perform lookups at the same time without interfering with or overwriting each other's results. The pages you see are your own.

*   [The Page Footer](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#the-page-footer)
    
*   [Page Command](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#page-command)
    
*   [Session-Specific Results](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages#session-specific-results)

---

# Restriction Types - Item Restrictions | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `types` array in your JSON file determines _what_ the player is prevented from doing when the conditions are met.

Available Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types#available-types "Direct link to Available Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Restriction Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `cant_craft` | Prevents the item from being crafted in a crafting table or player inventory. |
| `cant_smelt` | Prevents the item from being smelted in a furnace, blast furnace, or smoker. |
| `cant_brew` | Prevents the potion from being brewed in a brewing stand. |
| `cant_enchant` | Prevents the item from being enchanted in an enchanting table. |
| `cant_repair` | Prevents the item from being repaired or combined in an Anvil or Grindstone. |
| `cant_use_item` | Prevents the "right-click" usage of an item (e.g., eating food, throwing potions, using a bow). |
| `cant_break_block` | Prevents the player from breaking the target block, regardless of the tool held. |
| `cant_item_break_block` | Prevents the player from breaking a block _specifically_ with the item defined in the conditions. |
| `cant_place_block` | Prevents the block/item from being placed in the world. |
| `cant_hurt_entity` | Prevents the player from dealing damage to an entity using the restricted item. |
| `cant_interact_entity` | Prevents right-clicking on an entity (e.g., shearing a sheep, trading with a villager). |
| `cant_interact_block` | Prevents right-clicking a block (e.g., opening a chest, pressing a button). |

Error Messages[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types#error-messages "Direct link to Error Messages")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When a player attempts a restricted action, a translatable error message appears in the chat (red text).

*   Example: `You can't craft this item yet.`
*   Keys: `itemrestrictions.inventory.<restriction_type>`

These can be customized via a resource pack modifying the language files.

*   [Available Types](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types#available-types)
    
*   [Error Messages](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/creating-restrictions/restriction-types#error-messages)

---

# Lookup Command - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `lookup` command is GriefLogger's most powerful feature, providing a flexible and robust way to search the entire log database using a combination of filters. It's the essential tool for in-depth investigations that go beyond a single block.

### Command Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#command-syntax "Direct link to Command Syntax")

The basic structure of the command is as follows:

    /grieflogger lookup <filter1> [filter2] [filter3] ...

**Alias:** `/gl lookup`

*   **Permission:** Requires permission level 2 (standard for most server operators).
*   **Arguments:** You can provide one or more filters to narrow down your search. The command supports up to five filters at once.

🚨Important: Radius Filter is Required

To prevent accidental, performance-intensive queries across the entire database, the **`radius` filter is a mandatory component of any lookup command.** Executing a lookup without a defined radius would default to searching a tiny area at the world's origin (coordinates 0, 0, 0) and will likely return no useful results.

**Always include a `radius` filter in your lookups to define a search area.**

### How It Works[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

When you execute a `lookup` command, GriefLogger queries the database for all log entries that match **all** of the filters you provide.

For example, if you search for `user.Steve radius.10`, the command will only return logs that were created by the player named "Steve" AND occurred within a 10-block radius of your current position.

Smart Suggestions

GriefLogger provides excellent tab-completion for this command. As you type, it will suggest available filters. Once you select a filter (e.g., `action.`), it will then suggest valid values for that filter, making it easy to build complex queries without memorizing every option.

### Practical Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#practical-examples "Direct link to Practical Examples")

warning

When using a colon (`:`) or a comma (`,`) in filter values, make sure to wrap the entire value in quotes to ensure proper parsing.

Example:

    /gl lookup action.place_block "include.oak_log,stone" radius.20

    /gl lookup action.place_block "include.minecraft:oak_log,minecraft:stone" radius.20

Here are a few examples to showcase the power of the `lookup` command:

*   **Find who broke diamond ore in the area:**

    /gl lookup action.break_block include.diamond_ore radius.50

*   **See everything a specific player has done in the last hour within a 100-block radius:**

    /gl lookup user.Griefer42 time.1h radius.100

*   **Check for theft of valuable items from any container in a base:**

    /gl lookup action.remove_item "include.diamond,netherite_ingot" radius.30 time.6h

### Viewing Results[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#viewing-results "Direct link to Viewing Results")

The results of a lookup are displayed in your chat. Because a lookup can return thousands of entries, the results are always paginated. Use the `/gl page <number>` command to navigate through them. For more details, see the **[Pages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages)
** documentation.

*   [Command Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#command-syntax)
    
*   [How It Works](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#how-it-works)
    
*   [Practical Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#practical-examples)
    
*   [Viewing Results](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command#viewing-results)

---

# Configuration - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger's configuration allows you to manage its database connection, performance, and general settings.

Locating the Config File[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#locating-the-config-file "Direct link to Locating the Config File")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After running the server with GriefLogger for the first time, a configuration file will be generated at:

`config/grieflogger/grieflogger-server.yml`

You can edit this file with any text editor. Remember to restart your server for any changes to take effect.

Configuration Options[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options "Direct link to Configuration Options")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here are all the available options in `grieflogger-server.yml`, organized by section.

### Database[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#database "Direct link to Database")

This section controls how GriefLogger connects to its database. By default, it uses a local SQLite database, which requires no setup. For larger servers, MySQL/MariaDB is recommended for better performance.

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `useMysql` | Boolean | `false` | Set to `true` to use a MySQL/MariaDB database instead of SQLite. |
| `mysqlHost` | String | `"localhost"` | The IP address or hostname of your MySQL server. |
| `mysqlPort` | Integer | `3306` | The port your MySQL server is running on. |
| `mysqlDatabase` | String | `"database"` | The name of the database GriefLogger should use. |
| `mysqlUsername` | String | `"username"` | The username for the MySQL database connection. |
| `mysqlPassword` | String | `"password"` | The password for the MySQL database connection. |
| `mysqlTimeout` | Integer | `5000` | The connection timeout in milliseconds. |
| `useIndexes` | Boolean | `true` | Creates database indexes to significantly speed up lookup and inspection queries. It is highly recommended to keep this enabled. |

info

When `useMysql` is `false`, GriefLogger will automatically create and use a `database.db` file in the `config/grieflogger/` directory. All other `mysql*` settings are ignored in this case.

### General[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general "Direct link to General")

General settings for the mod.

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `maxPageSize` | Integer | `10` | The maximum number of log entries to display on a single page when using the `/gl lookup` or `/gl inspect` commands. |

### Server[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#server "Direct link to Server")

Settings related to server-side behavior.

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `serverSideOnlyMode` | Boolean | `true` | This mode ensures that all text components sent by the mod are processed server-side. It prevents issues for vanilla clients who do not have the mod installed. It is recommended to keep this enabled. |

### Queue[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#queue "Direct link to Queue")

Performance settings for the database query queue.

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `queueFrequency` | Integer | `20` | How often (in ticks) the mod sends batches of logged data to the database. `20` ticks is equal to 1 second. Lowering this value writes to the database more frequently, which may impact performance. |

### Hello[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#hello "Direct link to Hello")

Settings for maintaining the database connection.

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `helloFrequency` | Integer | `600` | How often (in ticks) a "hello" packet is sent to the database to keep the connection alive. This is primarily for preventing MySQL timeouts. `600` ticks is 30 seconds. |

*   [Locating the Config File](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#locating-the-config-file)
    
*   [Configuration Options](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options)
    *   [Database](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#database)
        
    *   [General](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general)
        
    *   [Server](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#server)
        
    *   [Queue](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#queue)
        
    *   [Hello](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration#hello)

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger provides detailed logs about how items are moved, created, and destroyed. This is especially useful for investigating theft from containers and tracking valuable resources.

* * *

Container Transactions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#container-transactions "Direct link to Container Transactions")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Names:** `ADD_ITEM`, `REMOVE_ITEM`

Instead of logging every single item click and drag, GriefLogger tracks the _net change_ of items inside a container. The log is generated at the moment a player closes a container interface.

*   **Trigger:** A player closes the GUI of a block entity with an inventory (e.g., Chest, Furnace, Hopper).
*   **What's Logged:** For each type of item, the log records the total amount added or removed by the player during that session. It includes the player, item type (with NBT data), item count, coordinates of the container, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

If a player opens a chest, takes 16 diamonds, and deposits 32 dirt, the logs will show:

    [Time] + [Player] added 32 dirt[Time] - [Player] removed 16 diamond

* * *

Dropping and Picking Up Items[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#dropping-and-picking-up-items "Direct link to Dropping and Picking Up Items")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Names:** `DROP_ITEM`, `PICKUP_ITEM`

These actions track items that enter or leave the world as item entities from a player's action.

*   **Trigger (`DROP_ITEM`):** A player drops an item from their inventory (e.g., by pressing 'Q' or dragging it out of the inventory screen).
*   **Trigger (`PICKUP_ITEM`):** A player walks over an item entity and picks it up.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the item and its count, the location where the action occurred, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-1 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] dropped 1 stone_sword[Time] + [Player] picked up 1 stone_sword

* * *

Crafting and Smelting[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#crafting-and-smelting "Direct link to Crafting and Smelting")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `CRAFT_ITEM`

This action is logged whenever a player creates a new item through crafting or smelting.

*   **Trigger:** A player takes a finished item from a crafting table output slot, or a furnace output slot.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the resulting item and its count, the location of the action, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-2 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] + [Player] crafted 4 oak_planks

* * *

Consuming Items[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#consuming-items "Direct link to Consuming Items")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `CONSUME_ITEM`

This is logged when a player finishes using an item that gets consumed, such as food or potions.

*   **Trigger:** A player finishes the "using" animation for a consumable item.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the item that was consumed, the player's location, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-3 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] consumed 1 cooked_beef

* * *

Breaking Items[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#breaking-items "Direct link to Breaking Items")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `BREAK_ITEM`

This action logs when a tool, weapon, or armor piece with durability runs out and is destroyed.

*   **Trigger:** An item's durability reaches zero through use.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the item that broke, the player's location, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-4 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] broke 1 iron_pickaxe

* * *

Throwing and Shooting Projectiles[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#throwing-and-shooting-projectiles "Direct link to Throwing and Shooting Projectiles")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Names:** `THROW_ITEM`, `SHOOT_ITEM`

GriefLogger distinguishes between thrown items (like snowballs) and shot projectiles (like arrows).

*   **Trigger (`THROW_ITEM`):** A player throws a projectile item like an Ender Pearl, Snowball, or Egg.
*   **Trigger (`SHOOT_ITEM`):** A player shoots an arrow from a Bow or Crossbow.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the projectile item, the player's location, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-5 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] shot 1 arrow[Time] - [Player] threw 1 ender_pearl

*   [Container Transactions](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#container-transactions)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output)
        
*   [Dropping and Picking Up Items](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#dropping-and-picking-up-items)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-1)
        
*   [Crafting and Smelting](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#crafting-and-smelting)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-2)
        
*   [Consuming Items](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#consuming-items)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-3)
        
*   [Breaking Items](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#breaking-items)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-4)
        
*   [Throwing and Shooting Projectiles](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#throwing-and-shooting-projectiles)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage#example-log-output-5)

On this page

While the restrictions themselves are defined in individual JSON files, the mod has a main configuration file located at:

`config/itemrestrictions/item-restrictions-common.yaml`

Configuration Options[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options "Direct link to Configuration Options")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Excluded Restrictions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/configuration#excluded-restrictions "Direct link to Excluded Restrictions")

Sometimes you may want to temporarily disable a restriction without deleting the file, or disable a restriction added by a modpack via a Datapack.

    restrictions:  # A list of restriction IDs to exclude from the game.  # Format: ['<namespace>:<restriction_id>']  excluded_restrictions:    - "itemrestrictions:diamond_sword_usage"    - "packname:disable_tnt"

*   [Configuration Options](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options)
    *   [Excluded Restrictions](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/getting-started/configuration#excluded-restrictions)

# Include Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `include` filter is the opposite of the `exclude` filter. It restricts the search to _only_ show results involving the specific blocks or items you list. This is the best way to search for actions related to valuable materials.

*   **Filter Name:** `include`
*   **Abbreviation:** `i`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The value is the Minecraft namespaced ID of the block or item you want to search for. The `minecraft:` prefix is optional. You can specify multiple items by separating them with a comma.

    include.<item_id>[,item_id]...

Filter Conflict

You cannot use the `include` and `exclude` filters in the same command, as they perform opposite functions.

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **Search for who has placed or broken diamond blocks in a 100-block radius:**

    /gl lookup include.diamond_block radius.100

*   **Track who has taken netherite items from chests in the last day in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup action.remove_item "include.netherite_sword,netherite_pickaxe,netherite_ingot" time.1d radius.50

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter#examples)

---

# Chat Commands - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

To provide a full context for player actions, GriefLogger records all public chat messages and executed commands. This is invaluable for moderation and understanding the events leading up to an incident.

* * *

Chat Messages[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands#chat-messages "Direct link to Chat Messages")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GriefLogger captures every message a player sends in the game's public chat.

*   **Trigger:** A player sends a chat message.
*   **What's Logged:** The player who sent the message, their location, the timestamp, and the full, unformatted content of the message string.

note

The chat history is not currently viewable via the in-game lookup commands but is stored in the `chats` table in the database for external review.

* * *

Commands[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands#commands "Direct link to Commands")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GriefLogger records every command a player attempts to execute, regardless of whether they have permission or if the command is successful.

*   **Trigger:** A player executes a command (a chat message starting with `/`).
*   **What's Logged:** The player who executed the command, their location, the timestamp, and the entire command string as it was typed.

This is extremely useful for auditing the use of potentially powerful commands.

note

Similar to chat, command history is stored in the `commands` table in the database for external review and is not currently part of the in-game lookup results.

*   [Chat Messages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands#chat-messages)
    
*   [Commands](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands#commands)

---

# Structure - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/structure#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:structure` condition type checks if the player is inside a specific structure.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/structure#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `structure` | `TagKey<Structure>` | ✅Required | \-  | Structure tag to check. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/structure#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives a reward for entering a village.

data/my\_datapack/arc/village\_reward.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_walk",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:structure",            "structure": "minecraft:village"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:exp",            "min": 1,            "max": 1        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/structure#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

----------------\

The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

The arc:onuseitem action type is triggered when a player uses an item (right-clicks with an item).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_use_item)

[🚶‍♂️ On Walk\
-------------\
\
The arc:on\_walk action type is triggered when a player is walking.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_walk)

# Riding Entity Type - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/riding_entity_type#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:riding_entity_type` condition type checks the type of entity the player is currently riding.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/riding_entity_type#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `entity_type` | `EntityType` | ✅Required | \-  | The entity type being ridden. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/riding_entity_type#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives speed when riding a pig.

data/my\_datapack/arc/fast\_pig.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_horse_ride",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:riding_entity_type",            "entity_type": "minecraft:pig"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:speed",                "duration": 20            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/riding_entity_type#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

![Grief Logger](https://daqem.com/img/projects/grieflogger.webp)

Grief Logger
============

A fast mod that uses SQLite or MySQL to log player interactions.

[](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mod-mods/grieflogger)
[](https://modrinth.com/mod/grieflogger)
[](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger)

👋Welcome!

Welcome to the official documentation for **GriefLogger**, your all-in-one logging and anti-griefing tool for Minecraft servers. This wiki contains everything you need to know to install, configure, and master the mod.

What is GriefLogger?[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#what-is-grieflogger "Direct link to What is GriefLogger?")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GriefLogger is a powerful server-side mod designed for administrators who need a clear and detailed record of player activity. It silently logs all significant player interactions, from breaking a block to taking an item from a chest, and stores this data in a robust database.

With its intuitive in-game tools, you can instantly investigate suspicious activity, roll back grief without a full server backup, resolve player disputes with concrete evidence, and maintain a secure and fair environment for your community.

* * *

Core Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#core-features "Direct link to Core Features")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GriefLogger is built to be both powerful and easy to use.

### 🔍 Comprehensive Logging

Tracks block placements/breaks, container transactions, item usage (crafting, dropping, consuming), player sessions, chat messages, and commands.

### 👆 In-Game Inspector Tool

Simply enable `/gl inspect` and click on any block to instantly view its history. The fastest way to check a specific location for suspicious activity.

### 🔎 Advanced Lookup Command

Use `/gl lookup` with a powerful set of filters (user, radius, time, action, item) to perform complex, database-wide searches for any event.

### 🗄️ Flexible Database Support

Works out-of-the-box with a simple file-based **SQLite** database, or can be configured to connect to a high-performance **MySQL/MariaDB** server for larger communities.

* * *

Where to Start[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#where-to-start "Direct link to Where to Start")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ready to protect your server? Follow these steps to get GriefLogger up and running.

1.  **[🚀 Installation](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation)
    :** A step-by-step guide to adding GriefLogger and its dependencies to your server.
2.  **[⚙️ Configuration](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/configuration)
    :** Learn how to tweak the settings, including how to connect to a MySQL database.
3.  **[🕵️‍♀️ Using the Inspector](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command)
    :** Dive right in and learn the easiest way to check block history.

* * *

Join the Community[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#join-the-community "Direct link to Join the Community")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Have a question, found a bug, or want to contribute? We'd love to hear from you.

*   **Found a bug?** Head over to our **[GitHub Issue Tracker](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/reporting-issues)
    ** to report it.
*   **Need help or want to chat?** Join our **[Discord Server](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/discord)
    **.
*   **Want to contribute?** Check out our **[Contributing Guide](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing)
    **.
*   **Love the mod?** You can **[support the developer on Ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/daqem)
    ** to help fund continued development.

*   [What is GriefLogger?](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#what-is-grieflogger)
    
*   [Core Features](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#core-features)
    
*   [Where to Start](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger#where-to-start)

# Launch Player - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/launch_player#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:launch_player` reward type applies a velocity vector to the player, launching them in a direction.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/launch_player#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `x` | `float` | ❌Optional | `0` | Force X. |
| `y` | `float` | ❌Optional | `0` | Force Y. |
| `z` | `float` | ❌Optional | `0` | Force Z. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/launch_player#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example launches the player into the air when they jump.

data/my\_datapack/arc/super\_jump.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_jump",    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:launch_player",            "y": 1.0        }    ]}

The arc:getattackspeed action type is used to retrieve the attack speed of a player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_attack_speed)

----------------------\
\
The arc:ongetdestroy\_speed action type is triggered when a player is determining how quickly they can break a block. This action type allows you to modify the destroy speed based on various conditions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_get_destroy_speed)

----------------------\

[💔 On Item Break\
----------------\
\
The arc:onitembreak action type is triggered when a tool or armor piece loses all durability and breaks.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_item_break)

----------\
\
The arc:on\_jump action type is triggered when a player jumps.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_jump)

The arc:onkillentity action type is triggered when a player kills an entity.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_kill_entity)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onpickupitem action type is triggered when a player picks up an item from the ground.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_pickup_item)

The arc:onplaceblock action type is triggered when a player places a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_place_block)

----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Enchant Item - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/enchant_item#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:enchant_item` reward type applies enchantments to an item in a specific slot.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/enchant_item#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `enchantments` | `ArcEnchantment[]` | ✅Required | \-No default value | List of enchantments to apply. |
| `target` | `ArcItemTarget` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Target item slot. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/enchant_item#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example enchants the held item with Sharpness 1 when killing a mob.

data/my\_datapack/arc/blood\_lust.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_kill_entity",    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:enchant_item",            "target": "MAIN_HAND",            "enchantments": [                {                    "id": "minecraft:sharpness",                    "level": 1                }            ]        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/enchant_item#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Drop Item - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/drop_item#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:drop_item` reward type drops an item at the location of the action (e.g., where a block was broken).

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/drop_item#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | [`ItemStack`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#item) | ✅Required | \-No default value | The item to drop. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/drop_item#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example drops a Golden Apple when breaking leaves.

data/my\_datapack/arc/apple\_drop.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:blocks",            "blocks": ["#minecraft:leaves"]        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:drop_item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:golden_apple",                "count": 1            }        }    ]}

As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. # Development - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger is an open-source project, and contributions from the community are welcome. Whether you're a developer looking to add a new feature, a translator helping to make the mod accessible to more people, or a user reporting a bug, your input is valuable.

### Project Philosophy[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development#project-philosophy "Direct link to Project Philosophy")

The goal is to create a reliable, performant, and user-friendly logging tool for server administrators. Development focuses on maintaining compatibility with popular mod loaders and providing powerful, intuitive tools for data investigation.

### Technology Stack[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development#technology-stack "Direct link to Technology Stack")

*   **Language:** Java 21
*   **Build Tool:** Gradle
*   **Framework:** Architectury (for Fabric & NeoForge multi-loader support)
*   **Supported Loaders:** Fabric, NeoForge

### How You Can Help[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development#how-you-can-help "Direct link to How You Can Help")

*   **[Contributing Code or Translations](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing)
    :** If you'd like to contribute directly to the project, this page has guidelines for setting up your environment and submitting your changes.
*   **[Reporting Issues](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/reporting-issues)
    :** A well-documented bug report is one of the most helpful contributions you can make.
*   **[Suggesting Features](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger/issues)
    :** Have an idea for a new feature? Open an issue on GitHub to start a discussion.

*   [Project Philosophy](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development#project-philosophy)
