# Block Interactions - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger tries to records every interaction a player has with the blocks in your world. This is crucial for identifying griefing, tracking build progress, and understanding how players interact with their environment.

* * *

Placing Blocks[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#placing-blocks "Direct link to Placing Blocks")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `PLACE_BLOCK`

This action is logged whenever a player places a block in the world. This includes standard blocks, as well as liquids placed from buckets.

*   **Trigger:** A player successfully places a block.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the type of block placed, the coordinates of the placement, and the timestamp.

Liquid Logging

Thanks to specific handling for `BucketItem`, GriefLogger also logs when a player places a liquid (like water or lava) as a `PLACE_BLOCK` action for the corresponding liquid source block.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

A log entry for placing a block will look similar to this when using the inspect or lookup commands:

    [Time] + [Player] placed stone

* * *

Breaking Blocks[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#breaking-blocks "Direct link to Breaking Blocks")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `BREAK_BLOCK`

This is the counterpart to placing blocks. It's logged whenever a player destroys a block.

*   **Trigger:** A player successfully breaks a block.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the type of block broken, the coordinates, and the timestamp.

Liquid Logging

Similarly to placing liquids, GriefLogger logs a `BREAK_BLOCK` event when a player picks up a liquid source with a bucket.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-1 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] broke dirt

* * *

Interacting with Blocks[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#interacting-with-blocks "Direct link to Interacting with Blocks")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `INTERACT_BLOCK`

This action is logged when a player right-clicks a "functional" or "intractable" block without placing another block against it. This helps track the usage of various devices and doors.

*   **Trigger:** A player right-clicks an interactive block.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, the type of block interacted with, the coordinates, and the timestamp.

The list of tracked intractable blocks is extensive and includes:

*   Fence Gates
*   Dispensers & Droppers
*   Note Blocks
*   Chests, Barrels, Shulker Boxes & Vaults
*   Furnaces (all types)
*   Levers & Buttons
*   Trapdoors & Doors
*   Brewing Stands
*   Redstone components like Repeaters
*   Hoppers
*   Utility blocks like Crafting Tables, Anvils, Grindstones, etc.
*   Signs, Beacons, Lecterns
*   And many more.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-2 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] clicked oak_door

* * *

Killing Entities[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#killing-entities "Direct link to Killing Entities")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `KILL_ENTITY`

While not strictly a "block" interaction, this event is categorized under the `BlockAction` enum in the mod's code. It is logged whenever a player is the direct cause of a living entity's death.

*   **Trigger:** A player kills a mob or another player.
*   **What's Logged:** The player who performed the action, the type of entity that was killed, the coordinates of the entity at the time of death, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-3 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

    [Time] - [Player] killed zombie

*   [Placing Blocks](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#placing-blocks)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output)
        
*   [Breaking Blocks](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#breaking-blocks)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-1)
        
*   [Interacting with Blocks](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#interacting-with-blocks)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-2)
        
*   [Killing Entities](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#killing-entities)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions#example-log-output-3)

# API - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Currently, GriefLogger **does not provide a formal, stable API** for other mods to interact with. The project is primarily focused on its functionality as a standalone server tool.

### Why is there no API?[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#why-is-there-no-api "Direct link to Why is there no API?")

*   **Stability:** Providing a stable API requires a significant commitment. The internal structure of the mod is subject to change as new features are added and existing ones are refactored. Exposing these internals as a public API would risk breaking other mods with each update.
*   **Focus:** The development focus is on the core features of logging and data retrieval for server administrators.

### Can I still interact with GriefLogger?[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#can-i-still-interact-with-grieflogger "Direct link to Can I still interact with GriefLogger?")

While not officially supported, developers are free to explore the mod's open-source code. If you need to add custom logging from your own mod, you can inspect the `Services` interface (`com.daqem.grieflogger.database.service.Services`) to see how data is inserted into the database.

**Example (for educational purposes only):**

    // WARNING: This is NOT a stable API and may break at any time.import com.daqem.grieflogger.database.service.Services;import com.daqem.grieflogger.model.action.BlockAction;// ...Services.BLOCK.insertMaterial(    player.getUUID(),    player.level().dimension().location().toString(),    pos,    "my_mod:custom_block",    BlockAction.INTERACT_BLOCK);

Use at Your Own Risk

Interacting with the mod's internals this way is not supported. Your mod may break with any GriefLogger update. You will be responsible for maintaining this integration yourself.

### The Future of the API[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#the-future-of-the-api "Direct link to The Future of the API")

If you are a developer and have a strong use case for a GriefLogger API, please **[open a feature request](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger/issues)
** on our GitHub repository. Describe what functionality you need and why. If there is sufficient community interest, the development of a formal API may be considered for a future release.

*   [Why is there no API?](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#why-is-there-no-api)
    
*   [Can I still interact with GriefLogger?](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#can-i-still-interact-with-grieflogger)
    
*   [The Future of the API](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/api#the-future-of-the-api)

---

# Item Restrictions | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

![Item Restrictions](https://daqem.com/img/projects/item-restrictions.webp)

Item Restrictions
=================

Data driven item restriction library based on Arc Lib.

[](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/item-restrictions)
[](https://modrinth.com/mod/item-restrictions)
[](https://github.com/DAQEM/ItemRestrictions)

**Item Restrictions** is a powerful, data-driven mod that allows server admins and modpack creators to restrict specific interactions with items and blocks based on custom conditions.

Whether you want to prevent players from using diamond tools until they reach a certain experience level, stop players from placing lava in the Overworld, or disable crafting of specific endgame items, Item Restrictions handles it all through a simple JSON format.

Key Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Data-Driven:** All restrictions are defined in JSON files. You can add them via **Datapacks** or the global **Config** folder.
*   **Arc Lib Integration:** Powered by [Arc Lib](https://daqem.com/projects/arc)
    , allowing you to use an extensive list of conditions (XP level, dimension, biome, advancements, etc.) to trigger restrictions.
*   **Granular Control:** Restrict specific actions like Crafting, Smelting, Brewing, Enchanting, Block Breaking, Entity Interaction, and more.
*   **Client & Server Sync:** Restrictions are synced to the client to provide visual feedback and prevent "ghost" item usage.

Dependencies[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions#dependencies "Direct link to Dependencies")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Item Restrictions requires the following mods:

*   [**Arc Lib**](https://daqem.com/projects/arc)
     - Provides the condition system and data handling.
*   [**YAML Config**](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config)
     - For global configuration.
*   [**Architectury API**](https://docs.architectury.dev/)
     - For cross-platform compatibility (Forge/Fabric/NeoForge).

*   [Key Features](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions#key-features)
    
*   [Dependencies](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions#dependencies)

# Item - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/item#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:item` reward type gives an item to the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/item#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | [`ItemStack`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#item) | ✅Required | \-No default value | The item to give. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/item#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives the player 5 Diamonds.

data/my\_datapack/arc/give\_diamonds.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_advancement",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:advancement",            "id": "minecraft:story/mine_diamond"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:diamond",                "count": 5            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/item#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

\

----------------------\

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Examples - Item Restrictions | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Here are several complete examples of restriction files. You can copy these into your `config/itemrestrictions/restrictions/` folder (ensure you create subfolders for namespaces, e.g., `config/itemrestrictions/restrictions/my_server/example.json`).

### 1\. Locked Diamond Sword (Job Level Requirement)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#1-locked-diamond-sword-job-level-requirement "Direct link to 1. Locked Diamond Sword (Job Level Requirement)")

Prevents crafting, using, or hurting entities with a Diamond Sword unless the player is at least level 30 Hunter.

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:diamond_sword"    },    "types": [        "cant_craft",        "cant_use_item",        "cant_hurt_entity",        "cant_repair",        "cant_enchant"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:diamond_sword"            }        },        {            "type": "jobsplus:job_level",            "inverted": true, // Restrict if NOT at level 30            "job": "jobsplus:hunter",            "level": 30        }    ]}

### 2\. No TNT in the Overworld[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#2-no-tnt-in-the-overworld "Direct link to 2. No TNT in the Overworld")

Prevents placing TNT blocks, but only if the player is in the Overworld.

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:tnt"    },    "types": [        "cant_place_block"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:tnt"            }        },        {            "type": "arc:dimension",            "dimension": "minecraft:overworld"        }    ]}

### 3\. Disable Brewing Speed Potions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#3-disable-brewing-speed-potions "Direct link to 3. Disable Brewing Speed Potions")

Prevents brewing any potion that contains the "Swiftness" effect.

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:potion",        "components": {            "minecraft:potion_contents": {                "potion": "minecraft:swiftness"            }        }    },    "types": [        "cant_brew"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:potion",                "components": {                    "minecraft:potion_contents": {                        "potion": "minecraft:swiftness"                    }                }            }        }    ]}

### 4\. Anti-Grief Tool[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#4-anti-grief-tool "Direct link to 4. Anti-Grief Tool")

Prevents breaking any block if the player is holding a specific "Admin" stick.

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:stick"    },    "types": [        "cant_item_break_block"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item_in_hand",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:stick",                "components": {                    "minecraft:display_name": {                        "name": "{\"text\":\"Admin Stick\",\"color\":\"red\",\"italic\":false}"                    }                }            }        }    ]}

### 5\. Grouping Items (Logical OR)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#5-grouping-items-logical-or "Direct link to 5. Grouping Items (Logical OR)")

Restrict multiple different items (stone and dirt) from being crafted in either the Overworld or Nether.

    {    "icon": {        "id": "minecraft:barrier"    },    "types": [        "cant_craft"    ],    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:items",            "items": [                "minecraft:stone", // Stone                "#minecraft:dirt" // dirt item tag, any dirt variant (coarse dirt, podzol, etc.)            ]        }        {            "type": "arc:or",            "conditions": [                {                    "type": "arc:dimension",                    "dimension": "minecraft:overworld"                },                {                    "type": "arc:dimension",                    "dimension": "minecraft:the_nether"                }            ]        }    ]}

*   [1\. Locked Diamond Sword (Job Level Requirement)](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#1-locked-diamond-sword-job-level-requirement)
    
*   [2\. No TNT in the Overworld](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#2-no-tnt-in-the-overworld)
    
*   [3\. Disable Brewing Speed Potions](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#3-disable-brewing-speed-potions)
    
*   [4\. Anti-Grief Tool](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#4-anti-grief-tool)
    
*   [5\. Grouping Items (Logical OR)](https://daqem.com/projects/item-restrictions/wiki/examples#5-grouping-items-logical-or)

On this page

The `arc:entity_on_fire` reward type sets the target entity on fire.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/entity_on_fire#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ticks` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The duration of the fire in ticks. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/entity_on_fire#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will set a zombie on fire for 5 seconds when the player hurts it.

data/my\_datapack/arc/flaming\_zombie\_hit.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_hurt_entity",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_type",            "entity_type": "minecraft:zombie"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_on_fire",            "ticks": 100        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

# Reporting Issues - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/reporting-issues#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

A good bug report is the first step to getting a bug fixed. By providing detailed and accurate information, you make it much easier for the developers to identify and solve the problem.

**Before you post, please [search the existing issues](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger/issues)
 to see if someone has already reported the same problem.**

### How to Create a Bug Report[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/reporting-issues#how-to-create-a-bug-report "Direct link to How to Create a Bug Report")

All bug reports should be submitted to our **[GitHub Issue Tracker](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger/issues)
**.

Follow these steps to create a high-quality report:

1.  **Create a New Issue:** Click the "New Issue" button on the issue tracker page.
    
2.  **Write a Clear Title:** Your title should be a brief, clear summary of the issue.
    

*   **Bad Title:** `Mod crashed`
*   **Good Title:** `[Crash] Server crashes when placing a barrel next to a hopper`

3.  **Provide Essential Information:** In the body of the issue, please include the following details. This is the most important part of your report.

*   **GriefLogger Version:** (e.g., `1.2.9`)
*   **Minecraft Version:** (e.g., `1.21.9`)
*   **Mod Loader:** Fabric or NeoForge, including its version number.
*   **Full Mod List:** A list of all other mods you have installed.
*   **Server Logs:** Copy the full crash report or the relevant section from your `logs/latest.log` file.
*   **Configuration File:** Copy the contents of your `grieflogger-server.yml` file.

Use a Paste Service

For long logs or configuration files, please use a service like [Pastebin](https://pastebin.com/)
, [Gist](https://gist.github.com/)
, or [mclo.gs](https://mclo.gs/)
 and link to it in your issue. Do **not** paste huge walls of text directly into the issue description.

Sanitize Your Data!

Before posting your configuration file, be sure to remove or redact sensitive information, such as your **`mysqlPassword`**.

4.  **Describe the Issue:**

*   **What happened?** A clear description of the bug.
*   **What did you expect to happen?** What the correct behavior should have been.
*   **Steps to Reproduce:** Provide a precise, step-by-step list of actions to trigger the bug. This is critical for debugging.

A detailed report will lead to a much faster resolution. Thank you for helping improve GriefLogger!

*   [How to Create a Bug Report](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/support/reporting-issues#how-to-create-a-bug-report)

---

On this page

The `arc:effect` reward type applies a potion effect to the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will give the player a Speed II effect for 10 seconds when they break any log.

data/my\_datapack/arc/speed\_on\_log\_break.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:blocks",            "blocks": ["#minecraft:logs"]        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": "minecraft:speed",            "duration": 200,            "amplifier": 1        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

# Installation - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger is a server-side mod. It only needs to be installed on the server. Clients do not need to have the mod installed to join.

Requirements[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before installing GriefLogger, please ensure you have the following:

*   [Architectury API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/architectury-api)
    
*   [Yaml Config](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/yaml-config)
    

Installation Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps "Direct link to Installation Steps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Download the Correct Files:** Download GriefLogger and its dependencies for your specific mod loader (Fabric or NeoForge) and Minecraft version.

*   **GriefLogger:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/grieflogger)
    , [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/8oGVUFuX)
    
*   **Architectury API:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/architectury-api)
    , [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/architectury-api)
    
*   **Yaml Config:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/yaml-config)
    , [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/yaml-config)
    

2.  **Install the Mod Loader:** Ensure you have the latest version of Fabric or NeoForge installed on your server.
    
3.  **Place Files in `mods` Folder:** Move the downloaded `.jar` files for GriefLogger, Architectury API, and Yaml Config into your server's `mods` folder. If the folder doesn't exist, create it.
    
4.  **Start Your Server:** Start your server. GriefLogger will generate its configuration file and create the necessary database files on the first run.
    

*   Fabric
*   NeoForge

**Your `mods` folder should look like this for a Fabric server:**

    /mods├── architectury-x.x.x-fabric.jar├── grieflogger-x.x.x-fabric.jar└── yaml-config-x.x.x-fabric.jar

**Your `mods` folder should look like this for a NeoForge server:**

    /mods├── architectury-x.x.x-neoforge.jar├── grieflogger-x.x.x-neoforge.jar└── yaml-config-x.x.x-neoforge.jar

*   [Requirements](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements)
    
*   [Installation Steps](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps)

---

# Inspect Command - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The Inspector is GriefLogger's most straightforward tool for investigating a specific location. It allows you to check the history of blocks and containers simply by interacting with them in the world.

### Toggling the Inspector[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#toggling-the-inspector "Direct link to Toggling the Inspector")

To enable or disable the Inspector, use the following command:

    /grieflogger inspect

**Alias:** `/gl inspect`

*   **Permission:** Requires permission level 2 (standard for most server operators).
*   **Functionality:** This command acts as a toggle. Executing it once enables Inspector mode. Executing it again disables it. You will receive a chat message confirming whether it has been enabled or disabled.

Gameplay Interruption

While the Inspector is active, your normal ability to break and interact with blocks is disabled. You will not be able to mine blocks or open chests. Remember to toggle it off with `/gl inspect` to resume normal gameplay.

### Using the Inspector[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#using-the-inspector "Direct link to Using the Inspector")

Once enabled, your clicks will query the database instead of performing their usual actions.

#### Left-Clicking a Block[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#left-clicking-a-block "Direct link to Left-Clicking a Block")

*   **Action:** Shows the **placement and break history** of the block you clicked.
*   **Use Case:** Find out who placed a specific block or who recently destroyed a block that used to be in that spot.

#### Right-Clicking a Block[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#right-clicking-a-block "Direct link to Right-Clicking a Block")

*   **Action:** Shows the **interaction history** of the block you clicked.
*   **Use Case:** See who has recently used a lever, opened a door, or accessed a crafting table.

### Special Cases[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#special-cases "Direct link to Special Cases")

GriefLogger has intelligent handling for more complex blocks:

*   **Containers (Chests, Barrels, Furnaces, etc.):** Right-clicking a container will show a history of all items added or removed from it, as well as who accessed it.
*   **Double Chests:** The Inspector is smart enough to know which two blocks form a double chest. Right-clicking one half will show the combined inventory transaction history for the entire double chest.
*   **Doors:** Clicking on either the top or bottom half of a door will show the interaction and placement/break history for the entire door structure.

### Viewing Results[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#viewing-results "Direct link to Viewing Results")

The results of an inspection are displayed directly in your chat. If the history is long, it will be broken into multiple pages. You can navigate these pages using the `/gl page` command. See the **[Pages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages)
** documentation for more details.

*   [Toggling the Inspector](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#toggling-the-inspector)
    
*   [Using the Inspector](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#using-the-inspector)
    *   [Left-Clicking a Block](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#left-clicking-a-block)
        
    *   [Right-Clicking a Block](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#right-clicking-a-block)
        
*   [Special Cases](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#special-cases)
    
*   [Viewing Results](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command#viewing-results)

# Filters - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Filters are the core component of the `/gl lookup` command. They are the criteria you define to narrow down your search and find the exact logs you need.

### Filter Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#filter-syntax "Direct link to Filter Syntax")

Every filter follows a simple `name.value` format.

*   **`name`**: The type of filter you want to apply (e.g., `user`, `radius`).
*   **`value`**: The specific data you are searching for (e.g., `Steve`, `10`).

For example, `user.Steve` is a filter that looks for actions performed by the player named Steve.

### Combining Filters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#combining-filters "Direct link to Combining Filters")

You can use up to five filters in a single `/gl lookup` command. The search will only return entries that match **all** of the specified filters. This allows you to construct highly precise queries.

**Example:** `/gl lookup user.Steve radius.20 action.place_block` This command searches for logs where:

1.  The user was **Steve**, AND
2.  The action was within a **20-block radius** of you, AND
3.  The action was a **`place_block`** event.

### Comma-Separated Values[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#comma-separated-values "Direct link to Comma-Separated Values")

Filters that accept specific names (like `user`, `action`, `include`, and `exclude`) allow you to provide multiple values separated by a comma, without any spaces.

**Example:** `/gl lookup "include.diamond_ore,gold_ore,ancient_debris"` This will search for logs related to any of those three block types.

### Available Filters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#available-filters "Direct link to Available Filters")

Click on any filter to learn more about its specific syntax and purpose.

*   **[Action Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter)
    **: Filters by the type of event (e.g., breaking a block, adding an item).
*   **[Exclude Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter)
    **: Excludes specific blocks or items from the results.
*   **[Include Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/include-filter)
    **: Restricts the search to only the specified blocks or items.
*   **[Radius Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter)
    **: Limits the search to a cubic area around you.
*   **[Time Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter)
    **: Searches for logs within a specific recent timeframe.
*   **[User Filter](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter)
    **: Filters by the player who performed the action.\`\`\`

*   [Filter Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#filter-syntax)
    
*   [Combining Filters](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#combining-filters)
    
*   [Comma-Separated Values](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#comma-separated-values)
    
*   [Available Filters](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters#available-filters)

# JSON Formats - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

This page details the specific JSON object structures and Enumeration values used throughout Arc's Action, Condition, and Reward files.

Complex Objects[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#complex-objects "Direct link to Complex Objects")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ArcBlockState[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcblockstate "Direct link to ArcBlockState")

Represents a block and its properties. It can be defined as a simple string (Resource Location) if no properties are needed, or as an object for more specific matching.

**As a String:**

    "minecraft:chest"

**As an Object:**

    {    "id": "minecraft:chest", // The block ID    "properties": {          // Optional: Simple map for equality checks        "facing": "north",        "waterlogged": "false"    }}

**Advanced Properties:** For advanced comparisons (like checking if a property is _not_ a value), `properties` can be a list of property objects:

    {    "id": "minecraft:chest",    "properties": [        {            "name": "facing",            "value": "south",            "comparison_type": "!="        }    ]}

### ArcItemStack[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcitemstack "Direct link to ArcItemStack")

Represents an item stack. Can be a simple string or a full object including counts and components.

**As a String:**

    "minecraft:diamond"

**As an Object:**

    {    "id": "minecraft:diamond_sword",    "count": 1, // Optional, defaults to 1    "components": { // Optional, standard Minecraft Data Components        "minecraft:enchantments": {            "levels": {                "minecraft:sharpness": 5            }        }    }}

### MobEffectInstance[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#mobeffectinstance "Direct link to MobEffectInstance")

Represents a potion effect applied to an entity.

**As a String:**

    "minecraft:speed"

**As an Object:**

    {    "id": "minecraft:speed",      // The effect ID    "amplifier": 0,               // Optional (0 = Level 1), default 0    "duration": 200,              // Optional, duration in ticks, default 200    "ambient": false,             // Optional, default false    "show_particles": true,       // Optional, default true    "show_icon": true             // Optional, default true}

### ArcEnchantment[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcenchantment "Direct link to ArcEnchantment")

Represents an enchantment and its level.

    {    "id": "minecraft:sharpness",    "level": 3}

### EntityDataProperty[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#entitydataproperty "Direct link to EntityDataProperty")

Used in the `arc:entity_data` condition to check specific dynamic values on an entity.

    {    "id": "arc:health",        // The data type ID (see Entity Data Registry below)    "value": "10",             // The value to compare against (as a string)    "comparison": ">="         // Optional comparison type, default "=="}

* * *

Enums & Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#enums--types "Direct link to Enums & Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ComparisonType[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#comparisontype "Direct link to ComparisonType")

Used in various conditions (like health, experience, block properties) to compare numerical values.

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `==` | Equal to |
| `!=` | Not equal to |
| `>` | Greater than |
| `<` | Less than |
| `>=` | Greater than or equal to |
| `<=` | Less than or equal to |

### InteractionHand[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#interactionhand "Direct link to InteractionHand")

Specifies which hand is being used or checked.

*   `MAIN_HAND` (or `main`)
*   `OFF_HAND` (or `off`)

### ArcItemTarget[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcitemtarget "Direct link to ArcItemTarget")

Specifies which equipment slot to target for rewards like `enchant_item` or `disarm_entity`.

*   `MAIN_HAND`
*   `OFF_HAND`
*   `HEAD`
*   `CHEST`
*   `LEGS`
*   `FEET`

### ArcPositionTarget[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcpositiontarget "Direct link to ArcPositionTarget")

Specifies the origin position for rewards like `spawn_entity`, `play_sound`, or `set_block`.

*   `PLAYER` (The player's current position)
*   `BLOCK` (The position of the block involved in the action)
*   `ENTITY` (The position of the target entity involved in the action)

### ArcWeatherType[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcweathertype "Direct link to ArcWeatherType")

Used in weather conditions and rewards.

*   `CLEAR`
*   `RAIN`
*   `THUNDER`

### SoundSource[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#soundsource "Direct link to SoundSource")

Standard Minecraft sound categories.

*   `MASTER`
*   `MUSIC`
*   `RECORDS`
*   `WEATHER`
*   `BLOCKS`
*   `HOSTILE`
*   `NEUTRAL`
*   `PLAYERS`
*   `AMBIENT`
*   `VOICE`

### EffectCategory[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#effectcategory "Direct link to EffectCategory")

Classifies potion effects.

*   `BENEFICIAL`
*   `HARMFUL`
*   `NEUTRAL`

### Entity Data Registry[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#entity-data-registry "Direct link to Entity Data Registry")

These are the valid IDs for `EntityDataProperty`.

**General Entity**

*   `arc:is_on_fire` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_crouching` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_sprinting` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_swimming` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_glowing` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_invisible` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_on_ground` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_in_water` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_in_lava` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_in_rain` (Boolean)
*   `arc:air_supply` (Integer)
*   `arc:fall_distance` (Float)
*   `arc:ticks_frozen` (Integer)
*   `arc:has_no_gravity` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_silent` (Boolean)
*   `arc:pose` (String)

**Living Entity**

*   `arc:health` (Float)
*   `arc:max_health` (Float)
*   `arc:absorption_amount` (Float)
*   `arc:is_baby` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_using_item` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_blocking` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_sleeping` (Boolean)
*   `arc:arrow_count` (Integer)
*   `arc:stinger_count` (Integer)
*   `arc:main_arm` (String)
*   `arc:is_fall_flying` (Boolean)

**Player**

*   `arc:score` (Integer)
*   `arc:experience_level` (Integer)
*   `arc:total_experience` (Integer)
*   `arc:experience_progress` (Float)
*   `arc:is_creative` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_spectator` (Boolean)
*   `arc:food_level` (Integer)
*   `arc:saturation_level` (Float)

**Mob / Animal / Villager**

*   `arc:has_no_ai` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_left_handed` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_aggressive` (Boolean)
*   `arc:can_pickup_loot` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_leashed` (Boolean)
*   `arc:has_target` (Boolean)
*   `arc:in_love_time` (Integer)
*   `arc:is_tame` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_sitting` (Boolean)
*   `arc:has_owner` (Boolean)
*   `arc:is_trading` (Boolean)
*   `arc:villager_profession` (String)
*   `arc:is_casting_spell` (Boolean)

*   [Complex Objects](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#complex-objects)
    *   [ArcBlockState](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcblockstate)
        
    *   [ArcItemStack](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcitemstack)
        
    *   [MobEffectInstance](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#mobeffectinstance)
        
    *   [ArcEnchantment](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcenchantment)
        
    *   [EntityDataProperty](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#entitydataproperty)
        
*   [Enums & Types](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#enums--types)
    *   [ComparisonType](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#comparisontype)
        
    *   [InteractionHand](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#interactionhand)
        
    *   [ArcItemTarget](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcitemtarget)
        
    *   [ArcPositionTarget](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcpositiontarget)
        
    *   [ArcWeatherType](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#arcweathertype)
        
    *   [SoundSource](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#soundsource)
        
    *   [EffectCategory](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#effectcategory)
        
    *   [Entity Data Registry](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats#entity-data-registry)

# Is Sneaking - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_sneaking#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:is_sneaking` condition type checks if the player is crouching/sneaking.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_sneaking#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_sneaking#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example cancels fall damage if the player lands while sneaking.

data/my\_datapack/arc/safe\_landing.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_land",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_sneaking"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:cancel_action"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_sneaking#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🏆 On Advancement\
-----------------\
\
The arc:on\_advancement action type is triggered when a player receives an advancement.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/advancements/on_advancement)

------------------------\
\
The arc:onblockwith\_shield action type is triggered when a player successfully blocks damage using a shield.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_block_with_shield)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onbreakblock action type is triggered when a player breaks a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_break_block)

------------------\
\
The arc:onbreedanimal action type is triggered when a player breeds two animals.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_breed_animal)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onbrewpotion action type is triggered when a player brews a potion.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_brew_potion)

----------------------\
\
The arc:onchangedimension action type is triggered when a player travels between dimensions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_change_dimension)

-----------------\
\
The arc:oncraftitem action type is triggered when a player crafts an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_craft_item)

------------\
\
The arc:on\_crouch action type is triggered when a player crouches (sneaks).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_crouch)

-----------\
\
The arc:on\_death action type is triggered when the player dies.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_death)

-----------\
\
The arc:on\_drink action type is triggered when a player drinks an item (e.g., potion, milk bucket, etc.).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_drink)

[🪣 On Drop Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:ondropitem action type is triggered when a player drops an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_drop_item)

----------\

The arc:on\_eat action type is triggered when a player eats an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_eat)

------------------\

\

------------------\
\
The arc:onharvestcrop action type is triggered when a player harvests a crop.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_harvest_crop)

[🐎 On Horse Ride\
----------------\

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Command - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:command` reward type executes a server command.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `command` | `string` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The command to execute. |

### Placeholders[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#placeholders "Direct link to Placeholders")

The command string can include placeholders that will be replaced with dynamic values from the action data:

*   `%player%`: The player's name.
*   `%uuid%`: The player's UUID.
*   `%world%`: The resource location of the player's current world/dimension.
*   `%player_location%`: The player's block coordinates (e.g., `100 64 -250`).
*   `%block_location%`: The coordinates of the block from the action data.
*   `%item%`: The resource location of the item from the action data.
*   `%item_stack%`: The resource location of the item from the item stack in the action data.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will say "Hello, world!" in chat whenever the player joins the game.

data/my\_datapack/arc/hello\_world\_on\_join.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_advancement",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:advancement",            "advancement": "minecraft:story/root"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:command",            "command": "say Hello, world! Welcome, %player%!"        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#parameters)
    *   [Placeholders](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#placeholders)
        
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/server/command#example)

---

# Create Explosion - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/create_explosion#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:create_explosion` reward type creates an explosion at the player's current location.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/create_explosion#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `radius` | `float` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Explosion radius. |
| `causes_fire` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `false` | Does it start fires. |
| `block_interaction` | `string` | ❌Optional | `NONE` | Explosion interaction (NONE, BLOCK, TNT, MOB). |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/create_explosion#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example creates a small, safe explosion when the player lands after a long fall.

data/my\_datapack/arc/explosive\_landing.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_land",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_data",            "properties": [                {                    "id": "arc:fall_distance",                    "value": "10",                    "comparison": ">="                }            ]        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:create_explosion",            "radius": 2.0,            "causes_fire": false,            "block_interaction": "NONE"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/create_explosion#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Inventory Full - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/inventory_full#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:inventory_full` condition type checks if the player's inventory has no empty slots.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/inventory_full#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/inventory_full#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example warns the player when their inventory is full after picking up an item.

data/my\_datapack/arc/inventory\_full\_warn.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_pickup_item",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:inventory_full"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:command",            "command": "tellraw %player% {\"text\":\"Your inventory is full!\",\"color\":\"red\"}"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/inventory_full#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Teleport Player - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/teleport_player#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:teleport_player` reward type teleports the player randomly within a specified radius.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/teleport_player#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `radius` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Radius to teleport within. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/teleport_player#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example randomly teleports the player when they eat a Chorus Fruit.

data/my\_datapack/arc/chorus\_teleport.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_eat",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": "minecraft:chorus_fruit"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:teleport_player",            "radius": 16        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/teleport_player#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

The arc:onplaceblock action type is triggered when a player places a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_place_block)

[🌱 On Plant Crop\
----------------\
\
The arc:onplantcrop action type is triggered when a player plants a crop.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_plant_crop)

[🎣 On Rod Reel In\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onrodreel\_in action type is triggered when a player reels in a fishing rod.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_rod_reel_in)

[🏹 On Shoot Projectile\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

---------------\
\
The arc:on\_sprint action type is triggered when a player is sprinting.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_sprint)

---------------------\
\

-------------------------\

The arc:onelytrafly\_start action type is triggered when a player starts flying with an elytra.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_elytra_fly_start)

# Is Sleeping - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_sleeping#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:is_sleeping` condition type checks if the player is currently sleeping in a bed.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_sleeping#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_sleeping#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example heals the player while they are sleeping (e.g. triggered by a timer or update).

data/my\_datapack/arc/regen\_sleep.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_add_effect",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_sleeping"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:heal",            "amount": 1.0        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_sleeping#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Learn More

[🕒 Change Time\
--------------\
\
The arc:change\_time reward type changes the time of day in the world.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_time)

[☀️ Change Weather\
-----------------\
\
The arc:change\_weather reward type sets the current weather in the world.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_weather)

[💥 Create Explosion\
-------------------\
\
The arc:create\_explosion reward type creates an explosion at the player's current location.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/create_explosion)

[🔊 Play Sound\
-------------\
\
The arc:play\_sound reward type plays a specific sound event at a target location.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/play_sound)

[⚡ Strike Lightning\
------------------\
\
The arc:strike\_lightning reward type summons a lightning bolt at a target location.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/strike_lightning)

# Experience - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:exp` reward type gives the player a specified range of experience points.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `min` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The minimum amount of experience to give. |
| `max` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The maximum amount of experience to give. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will give the player between 10 and 20 experience points every time they craft an item.

data/my\_datapack/arc/xp\_for\_crafting.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_craft_item",    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:exp",            "min": 10,            "max": 20        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp#example)

---

# Destroy Speed Multiplier - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/blocks/destroy_speed_multiplier#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:destroy_speed_multiplier` reward type modifies the speed at which a player breaks a block.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/blocks/destroy_speed_multiplier#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `multiplier` | `float` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The multiplier for destroy speed. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/blocks/destroy_speed_multiplier#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will make the player break obsidian five times faster.

data/my\_datapack/arc/fast\_obsidian.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_get_destroy_speed",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:block",            "block": "minecraft:obsidian"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:destroy_speed_multiplier",            "multiplier": 5.0        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/blocks/destroy_speed_multiplier#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/blocks/destroy_speed_multiplier#example)

# Contributing - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

We appreciate you taking the time to contribute to GriefLogger! Following these guidelines helps maintain a clear and efficient development process.

### Setting Up Your Development Environment[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#setting-up-your-development-environment "Direct link to Setting Up Your Development Environment")

1.  **Fork the Repository:** Start by forking the [official GriefLogger repository](https://github.com/DAQEM/GriefLogger)
     on GitHub to your own account.
    
2.  **Clone Your Fork:** Clone your forked repository to your local machine.
    

    git clone https://github.com/YourUsername/GriefLogger.gitcd GriefLogger

3.  **Ensure Correct Java Version:** The project requires **Java 21 (Temurin/OpenJDK)**. Make sure this is the JDK your system uses for Gradle.
    
4.  **Build the Project:** Run the `build` task using the Gradle wrapper. This will download all necessary dependencies and set up the project.
    

    ./gradlew build

This command will build both the Fabric and NeoForge versions of the mod.

### Making Changes[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#making-changes "Direct link to Making Changes")

1.  **Create a New Branch:** Always create a new branch for your changes. Base your branch off the `-dev` branch for the relevant Minecraft version (e.g., `1.21.9-dev`).

    git checkout -b feature/my-awesome-feature 1.21.9-dev

2.  **Write Your Code:** Implement your feature or bug fix. Try to adhere to the existing code style and structure.

### Submitting a Pull Request[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#submitting-a-pull-request "Direct link to Submitting a Pull Request")

1.  **Push to Your Fork:** Commit your changes and push your branch to your forked repository on GitHub.
    
2.  **Create a Pull Request:** From your fork's page on GitHub, create a new Pull Request.
    

*   **Target Branch:** Ensure your pull request is targeting the correct `-dev` branch (e.g., `1.21.9-dev`) of the main DAQEM/GriefLogger repository.
*   **Title & Description:** Provide a clear title and a detailed description of the changes you've made and why. If it resolves an existing issue, be sure to mention it (e.g., `Fixes #123`).

info

Your PR will automatically be tested by the GitHub Actions workflow. All checks must pass before it can be merged. A project maintainer will review your code, provide feedback, and merge it when it's ready.

*   [Setting Up Your Development Environment](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#setting-up-your-development-environment)
    
*   [Making Changes](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#making-changes)
    
*   [Submitting a Pull Request](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/development/contributing#submitting-a-pull-request)

---

# Is Smoking Recipe - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:is_smoking_recipe` condition type checks if a recipe is specifically for a smoker.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will give the player an extra cooked beef every time they smelt beef in a smoker.

data/my\_datapack/arc/extra\_beef\_from\_smoker.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_smelt_item",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_smoking_recipe"        },        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:cooked_beef" }        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:cooked_beef" }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🛠️ On Craft Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:oncraftitem action type is triggered when a player crafts an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_craft_item)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#example)
    
*   [Compatible Action Types](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_smoking_recipe#compatible-action-types)

On this page

The `arc:is_underwater` condition type checks if the player is fully submerged in water.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_underwater#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_underwater#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives water breathing when the player eats kelp underwater.

data/my\_datapack/arc/kelp\_breath.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_eat",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": "minecraft:dried_kelp"        },        {            "type": "arc:is_underwater"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:water_breathing",                "duration": 200            }        }    ]}

# Time Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `time` filter allows you to search for logs that occurred within a specific timeframe, counting backward from the moment you execute the command.

*   **Filter Name:** `time`
*   **Abbreviation:** `t`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The syntax is a number followed by a single-letter unit identifier.

    time.<number><unit>

### Available Units[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#available-units "Direct link to Available Units")

| Unit | Identifier |
| --- | --- |
| Minutes | `m` |
| Hours | `h` |
| Days | `d` |
| Years | `y` |

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **Find all container transactions in the last 90 minutes in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup "action.add_item,remove_item" time.90m radius.50

*   **See who has joined the server in the last 3 days in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup action.join time.3d radius.50

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Available Units](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#available-units)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/time-filter#examples)

# Player Actions - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger's primary function is to create a detailed, queryable record of actions performed by players on your server. This comprehensive logging covers a wide range of interactions, allowing server administrators to investigate incidents, resolve disputes, and understand exactly what happened in their world.

Every log entry is timestamped and associated with a specific player and location, providing a complete picture of the event.

This section is broken down into the different categories of actions that GriefLogger records. Explore each page to understand the specifics of what is logged.

### Categories of Logged Actions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions#categories-of-logged-actions "Direct link to Categories of Logged Actions")

[🧱 Block Interactions\
---------------------\
\
Detailed information on how GriefLogger tracks player interactions with blocks in the world.](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/block-interactions)

[💎 Item Usage\
-------------\
\
Monitors how players manage and use items, including container transactions, crafting, dropping, and consuming items.](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/item-usage)

[➡️ Player Sessions\
------------------\
\
Records when players join and leave the server, providing a clear timeline of player activity.](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions)

[💬 Chat & Commands\
------------------\
\
Logs all player chat messages and executed commands for moderation and auditing purposes.](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/chat-commands)

*   [Categories of Logged Actions](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions#categories-of-logged-actions)

# Exclude Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `exclude` filter is used to remove results involving specific blocks or items from your search. This is useful for filtering out common, "spammy" blocks like dirt and stone to focus on more valuable materials.

*   **Filter Name:** `exclude`
*   **Abbreviation:** `e`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The value is the Minecraft namespaced ID of the block or item you wish to exclude. The `minecraft:` prefix is optional. You can specify multiple items by separating them with a comma.

    exclude.<item_id>[,item_id]...

Filter Conflict

You cannot use the `exclude` and `include` filters in the same command, as they perform opposite functions.

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **Find all blocks broken by a player, except for common stone types in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup user.MinerJoe action.break_block "exclude.stone,dirt,cobblestone,gravel" radius.50

*   **Check container interactions in a vault, ignoring cobblestone removal:**

    /gl lookup radius.20 action.remove_item exclude.cobblestone

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/exclude-filter#examples)
