# Radius Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `radius` filter limits your search to a specific area around your character's current position. The search area is a cube, not a sphere.

*   **Filter Name:** `radius`
*   **Abbreviation:** `r`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The value is a single integer representing the distance from the player in all six directions (positive/negative X, Y, and Z).

    radius.<number>

For example, `radius.10` will search in a 21x21x21 cube centered on you (10 blocks in the positive direction, 10 blocks in the negative direction, and the block you are on for each axis).

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **See all player activity within a 5-block radius of your current location:**

    /gl lookup radius.5

*   **Find out who placed the TNT near you in the last 30 minutes:**

    /gl lookup include.tnt time.30m radius.25

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/radius-filter#examples)

# Action Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `action` filter allows you to search for specific types of events that have been logged. This is one of the most powerful filters for narrowing down your investigation.

*   **Filter Name:** `action`
*   **Abbreviation:** `a`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The value is the name of the action you want to find. You can specify multiple actions by separating them with a comma.

    action.<action_name>[,action_name]...

### Available Actions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#available-actions "Direct link to Available Actions")

The list of actions is extensive. Here are some of the most common ones:

*   **Block Actions:**
*   `place_block`
*   `break_block`
*   `interact_block`
*   `kill_entity`
*   **Item Actions (Containers):**
*   `add_item`
*   `remove_item`
*   **Item Actions (Player):**
*   `drop_item`
*   `pickup_item`
*   `craft_item`
*   `consume_item`
*   **Session Actions:**
*   `join`
*   `quit`

Use Tab-Completion!

The game's tab-completion will show you all available actions when you type `action.` and press Tab, so you don't need to memorize them.

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **Find all blocks broken or placed in your area:**

    /gl lookup "action.break_block,place_block" radius.15

*   **See who has joined or left the server in the last 10 minutes in a 100-block radius:**

    /gl lookup "action.join,quit" time.10m radius.100

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Available Actions](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#available-actions)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/action-filter#examples)

# Database - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

At its core, GriefLogger is a powerful data logging tool that relies on a database to store all the information it collects. Understanding how the database works will help you manage your server's data effectively, ensure good performance, and even perform advanced data analysis if you choose.

GriefLogger offers two distinct database solutions to fit the needs of any server, from a small private server for friends to a large public community.

### Database Options[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database#database-options "Direct link to Database Options")

*   **[SQLite](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/sqlite)
    **
    
*   **Default, Zero-Configuration:** This is the option GriefLogger uses out of the box. It stores all data in a single file (`database.db`) within your server's configuration folder.
    
*   **Best for:** Small to medium-sized servers, or for server owners who want maximum simplicity and do not want to manage an external database server.
    
*   **[MySQL / MariaDB](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql)
    **
    
*   **High-Performance, Scalable:** This option allows GriefLogger to connect to an external MySQL or MariaDB server. It is the recommended choice for large servers with high player counts.
    
*   **Best for:** Large, active communities where database performance and scalability are critical. This requires setting up and managing a separate database server.
    

Choose the sub-page that best fits your server's needs to learn more about setup and management.

*   [Database Options](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database#database-options)

---

# MySQL - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

For larger servers, connecting GriefLogger to a MySQL or MariaDB database is highly recommended for optimal performance and scalability. This guide will walk you through the process of configuring the connection.

### Prerequisites[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

Before you begin, you must have:

1.  A running MySQL or MariaDB server that your Minecraft server can connect to.
2.  A database (also known as a schema) created on the MySQL server for GriefLogger to use.
3.  A user account on the MySQL server with full permissions (`SELECT`, `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`, `CREATE`, `ALTER`, etc.) for that specific database.

note

The setup of a MySQL server is beyond the scope of this wiki, but there are many excellent tutorials available online. Popular choices include setting it up on the same machine as your Minecraft server or using a dedicated hosting provider.

### Configuration Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#configuration-steps "Direct link to Configuration Steps")

1.  **Locate the Configuration File:** Navigate to your server's configuration directory and open `config/grieflogger/grieflogger-server.yml` with a text editor.
    
2.  **Enable MySQL:** Find the `useMysql` setting and change its value from `false` to `true`.
    

        useMysql: true

3.  **Enter Connection Details:** Fill in the `mysqlHost`, `mysqlPort`, `mysqlDatabase`, `mysqlUsername`, and `mysqlPassword` fields with the details from your MySQL server setup.

*   `mysqlHost`: The IP address or domain name of your database server. Use `127.0.0.1` if it's on the same machine.
*   `mysqlPort`: The port your database server is listening on. The default for MySQL is `3306`.
*   `mysqlDatabase`: The name of the database you created for GriefLogger.
*   `mysqlUsername`: The username of the account with permissions for the database.
*   `mysqlPassword`: The password for that user account.

### Example Configuration[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#example-configuration "Direct link to Example Configuration")

Here is a complete example of what the `database` section should look like:

config/grieflogger/grieflogger-server.yml

    database:  # Whether to use MySQL or SQLite  useMysql: true  # MySQL host  mysqlHost: '127.0.0.1'  # MySQL port  mysqlPort: 3306  # MySQL database  mysqlDatabase: 'grieflogger_db'  # MySQL username  mysqlUsername: 'gl_user'  # MySQL password  mysqlPassword: 'a_very_secure_password'  # MySQL timeout  mysqlTimeout: 5000  # Whether to use indexes (improves inspect/lookup speed)  useIndexes: true

4.  **Restart Your Server:** Save the configuration file and restart your Minecraft server. Check the server console for any connection error messages. If the connection is successful, GriefLogger will automatically create the necessary tables (`blocks`, `users`, `containers`, etc.) in the database you specified.

### Troubleshooting[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

*   Connection Refused
*   Access Denied

If you see a "Connection Refused" error, it usually means one of three things:

*   The `mysqlHost` IP address is incorrect.
*   The `mysqlPort` is incorrect.
*   A firewall on your database server or Minecraft server is blocking the connection on that port.

An "Access Denied" error means your credentials are wrong.

*   Double-check your `mysqlUsername` and `mysqlPassword`.
*   Ensure the MySQL user is allowed to connect from the Minecraft server's IP address. Many MySQL setups default to only allowing connections from `localhost`. You may need to configure the user to be accessible from `'%'` (any host) or your server's specific IP.
*   Verify the user has sufficient permissions for the specified `mysqlDatabase`.

*   [Prerequisites](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#prerequisites)
    
*   [Configuration Steps](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#configuration-steps)
    
*   [Example Configuration](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#example-configuration)
    
*   [Troubleshooting](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/database/mysql#troubleshooting)

---

# Inspecting & Lookup - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger is more than just a data recorder; it provides powerful in-game tools to access and analyze the logs without ever having to leave Minecraft. This allows for rapid investigation of incidents as they happen.

There are two primary methods for querying the database in-game:

### The Inspector Tool[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup#the-inspector-tool "Direct link to The Inspector Tool")

*   **Command:** `/gl inspect`
*   **Purpose:** A simple, intuitive tool for quickly checking the history of a specific block or container.
*   **How it works:** After enabling the inspector, you simply left-click or right-click on blocks in the world to retrieve their history. It's perfect for quick, localized investigations.

### The Lookup Command[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup#the-lookup-command "Direct link to The Lookup Command")

*   **Command:** `/gl lookup`
*   **Purpose:** An advanced, filter-based search engine for the entire log database.
*   **How it works:** You provide a set of search criteria, called "filters," to find all log entries that match your query. This is the tool for complex investigations, such as finding all actions a specific player has taken within a certain time frame and area.

* * *

This section will guide you through the details of each command, the powerful filtering system, and how to navigate the results.

*   **[Inspect Command](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/inspect-command)
    **: Learn how to use the simple point-and-click inspection tool.
*   **[Lookup Command](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/lookup-command)
    **: Master the advanced search command for complex queries.
*   **[Filters](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters)
    **: Discover all the available filters to narrow down your search results.
*   **[Pages](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/pages)
    **: Understand how to navigate through multi-page search results.

*   [The Inspector Tool](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup#the-inspector-tool)
    
*   [The Lookup Command](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup#the-lookup-command)

On this page

The `arc:is_blasting_recipe` condition type checks if a recipe is specifically for a blast furnace.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_blasting_recipe#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_blasting_recipe#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will give the player a piece of coal every time they smelt an item in a blast furnace.

data/my\_datapack/arc/coal\_for\_blasting.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_smelt_item",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_blasting_recipe"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:coal" }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_blasting_recipe#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🛠️ On Craft Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:oncraftitem action type is triggered when a player crafts an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_craft_item)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/recipes/is_blasting_recipe#parameters)

The arc:on\_death action type is triggered when the player dies.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_death)

-----------\
\
The arc:on\_drink action type is triggered when a player drinks an item (e.g., potion, milk bucket, etc.).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_drink)

[🪣 On Drop Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:ondropitem action type is triggered when a player drops an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_drop_item)

\
The arc:on\_eat action type is triggered when a player eats an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_eat)

----------------\
\
The arc:onelytrafly action type is triggered when a player is flying with an elytra.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_elytra_fly)

[🪣 On Empty Bucket\
------------------\
\
The arc:onemptybucket action type is triggered when a player places a liquid or fish from a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_empty_bucket)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onenchantitem action type is triggered when a player enchants an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_enchant_item)

--------------------\
\
The arc:onfishedup\_item action type is triggered when a player fishes up an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_fished_up_item)

[⚔️ On Get Attack Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:getattackspeed action type is used to retrieve the attack speed of a player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_attack_speed)

[⚡ On Get Destroy Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:ongetdestroy\_speed action type is triggered when a player is determining how quickly they can break a block. This action type allows you to modify the destroy speed based on various conditions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_get_destroy_speed)

--------------\
\
The arc:ongethurt action type is triggered when a player takes damage.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_hurt)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onhurtplayer action type is triggered when a player hurts another player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_hurt_player)

---------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractblock action type is triggered when a player interacts with a block, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_interact_block)

[🖱️ On Interact Entity\
----------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractentity action type is triggered when a player interacts with an entity, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_interact_entity)

[💔 On Item Break\
----------------\
\
The arc:onitembreak action type is triggered when a tool or armor piece loses all durability and breaks.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_item_break)

[🦘 On Jump\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_jump action type is triggered when a player jumps.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_jump)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onkillentity action type is triggered when a player kills an entity.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_kill_entity)

----------\
\
The arc:on\_land action type is triggered when a player lands on the ground after falling or jumping.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_land)

[🤲 On Pickup Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onpickupitem action type is triggered when a player picks up an item from the ground.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_pickup_item)

[🧱 On Place Block\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onplaceblock action type is triggered when a player places a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_place_block)

[🌱 On Plant Crop\
----------------\
\
The arc:onplantcrop action type is triggered when a player plants a crop.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_plant_crop)

[🎣 On Rod Reel In\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onrodreel\_in action type is triggered when a player reels in a fishing rod.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_rod_reel_in)

[🏹 On Shoot Projectile\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

[🏃‍♂️ On Sprint\
---------------\
\
The arc:on\_sprint action type is triggered when a player is sprinting.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_sprint)

---------------------\

----------------\
\

---------------\
\
The arc:ontillsoil action type is triggered when a player uses a hoe on a dirt-like block to create farmland.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_till_soil)

[🤝 On Trade With Villager\
-------------------------\
\
The arc:ontradewith\_villager action type is triggered when a player successfully completes a trade with a Villager or Wandering Trader.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_trade_with_villager)

---------------\
\

---------------\
\

-------------\
\
The arc:on\_walk action type is triggered when a player is walking.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_walk)

# Effects - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

[🚿 Cleanse Effects\
------------------\
\
The arc:cleanse\_effects reward type removes specific categories of potion effects from the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/cleanse_effects)

[➕💉 Effect Amplifier Addition\
-----------------------------\
\
The arc:effectamplifieraddition reward type increases the amplifier of an existing effect on the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_amplifier_addition)

[⏳💉 Effect Duration Multiplier\
------------------------------\
\
The arc:effectdurationmultiplier reward type multiplies the duration of an effect a player receives.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier)

[💉 Potion Effect\
----------------\
\
The arc:effect reward type applies a potion effect to the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect)

[❌💉 Remove Effect\
-----------------\
\
The arc:remove\_effect reward type removes a specified effect from the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/remove_effect)

# Scoreboard - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/scoreboard/scoreboard#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:scoreboard` condition type checks if a player's score in a specific objective is within a given range.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/scoreboard/scoreboard#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `objective` | `string` | ✅Required | \-  | The scoreboard objective to check. |
| `min` | `int` | ✅Required | \-  | The minimum score. |
| `max` | `int` | ✅Required | \-  | The maximum score. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/scoreboard/scoreboard#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example rewards a player with a diamond if they break a block while their `kills` score is between 10 and 20.

data/my\_datapack/arc/reward\_for\_kill\_milestone.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:scoreboard",            "objective": "kills",            "min": 10,            "max": 20        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond" }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/scoreboard/scoreboard#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# saturation_level - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

.--- title: Saturation Level[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level#---title-saturation-level "Direct link to .---
title: Saturation Level")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `arc:saturation_level` condition type checks the player's current saturation level.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `saturation_level` | `float` | ✅Required | \-  | Saturation level value. |
| `comparison` | `ComparisonType` | ❌Optional | `==` | Comparison type. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives speed if the player is fully saturated (saturation >= 20).

data/my\_datapack/arc/full\_belly\_run.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_sprint",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:saturation_level",            "saturation_level": 20,            "comparison": ">="        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:speed",                "duration": 40            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Feed - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/feed#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:feed` reward type restores hunger and saturation to the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/feed#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `food_level` | `int` | ❌Optional | `0` | Food to add. |
| `saturation` | `float` | ❌Optional | `0` | Saturation to add. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/feed#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example feeds the player 2 hunger points when they kill an animal.

data/my\_datapack/arc/carnivore.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_kill_entity",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_types",            "entity_types": ["minecraft:pig", "minecraft:cow", "minecraft:chicken"]        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:feed",            "food_level": 2,            "saturation": 1.0        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/feed#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

\
The arc:onitembreak action type is triggered when a tool or armor piece loses all durability and breaks.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_item_break)

----------------------\

The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Change Time - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_time#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:change_time` reward type changes the time of day in the world.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_time#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `time` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Time to set or add. |
| `add` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `false` | Add time instead of setting. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_time#example "Direct link to Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example advances time by 1000 ticks when the player sleeps.

data/my\_datapack/arc/sleep\_fast\_forward.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_add_effect",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_sleeping"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:change_time",            "time": 1000,            "add": true        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_time#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

# Move to Entity - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/move_to_entity#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:move_to_entity` reward type pulls the player towards the target entity's location.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/move_to_entity#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `force` | `float` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The force to move the player with. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/move_to_entity#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will pull the player towards a cow when they reel in a fishing rod that has hooked the cow.

data/my\_datapack/arc/grappling\_hook\_cow.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_rod_reel_in",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_type",            "entity_type": "minecraft:cow"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:move_to_entity",            "force": 1.5        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/move_to_entity#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/move_to_entity#example)

---

# Pull Entity - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/pull_entity#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:pull_entity` reward type pulls the target entity towards the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/pull_entity#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `force` | `double` | ❌Optional | `1` | Force of pull. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/pull_entity#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example pulls enemies closer when hitting them while sneaking.

data/my\_datapack/arc/magnet\_strike.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_hurt_entity",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:is_sneaking"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:pull_entity",            "force": 0.5        }    ]}

# Dimension - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/dimension#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:dimension` condition type checks if the player is in a specific dimension.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/dimension#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dimension` | `ResourceKey<Level>` | ✅Required | \-  | The dimension to check. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/dimension#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives the player a netherrack item every time they break a block in The Nether.

data/my\_datapack/arc/nether\_break\_reward.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:dimension",            "dimension": "minecraft:the_nether"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:netherrack" }        }    ]}

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

--------------\
\
The arc:pull\_entity reward type pulls the target entity towards the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/pull_entity)

[💨 Push Entity\
--------------\
\
The arc:push\_entity reward type pushes the target entity away from the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/push_entity)

[🧟 Spawn Entity\
---------------\
\
The arc:spawn\_entity reward type spawns a specified entity near the player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/spawn_entity)

# Player Sessions - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

GriefLogger tracks when players join and leave the server. This provides a simple but effective way to see who was online at any given time, which can be crucial context when investigating an incident.

* * *

Player Join[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#player-join "Direct link to Player Join")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `JOIN`

A `JOIN` action is logged the moment a player successfully connects to the server and enters the world.

*   **Trigger:** A player joins the server.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, their location upon joining, and the timestamp.

Additionally, every time a player joins, GriefLogger updates its internal user database with the player's current username to keep records accurate even if a player changes their name.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#example-log-output "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

When viewing logs, a join event will appear as:

    [Time] + [Player] joined

* * *

Player Quit[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#player-quit "Direct link to Player Quit")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Action Name:** `QUIT`

A `QUIT` action is logged when a player disconnects from the server, whether intentionally or due to a lost connection.

*   **Trigger:** A player leaves the server.
*   **What's Logged:** The player, their location at the moment of disconnecting, and the timestamp.

#### Example Log Output:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#example-log-output-1 "Direct link to Example Log Output:")

A quit event will appear as:

*   [Player Join](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#player-join)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#example-log-output)
        
*   [Player Quit](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#player-quit)
    *   [Example Log Output:](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/player-actions/player-sessions#example-log-output-1)

---

# User Filter - Grief Logger | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `user` filter is used to search for actions performed by one or more specific players.

*   **Filter Name:** `user`
*   **Abbreviation:** `u`

### Syntax[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter#syntax "Direct link to Syntax")

The value is the in-game name of a player. You can list multiple players by separating their names with a comma.

    user.<player_name>[,player_name]...

Username Database

GriefLogger maintains a database of user UUIDs and their last known usernames. This means you can still look up a player's logs even if they have since changed their Minecraft name.

### Examples[​](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter#examples "Direct link to Examples")

*   **Get a complete log of all actions by player `Notch` in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup user.Notch radius.50

*   **Find out if `Player1` or `Player2` broke any blocks in the last day in a 50-block radius:**

    /gl lookup "user.Player1,Player2" action.break_block time.1d radius.50

*   [Syntax](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter#syntax)
    
*   [Examples](https://daqem.com/projects/grieflogger/wiki/inspecting-lookup/filters/user-filter#examples)

---

# Remove Effect - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:remove_effect` reward type removes a specified effect from the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/remove_effect#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This reward type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/remove_effect#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will instantly remove any Poison effect the player receives.

data/my\_datapack/arc/poison\_immunity.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_add_effect",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": "minecraft:poison"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:remove_effect"        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/remove_effect#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/remove_effect#example)

# Experience Multiplier - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp_multiplier#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:exp_multiplier` reward type multiplies the amount of experience dropped from an action.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp_multiplier#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `multiplier` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The multiplier for the experience. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp_multiplier#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will double the experience dropped when a player breaks a coal ore block.

data/my\_datapack/arc/double\_xp\_from\_coal.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:block",            "block": "minecraft:coal_ore"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:exp_multiplier",            "multiplier": 2        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp_multiplier#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/experience/exp_multiplier#example)

# Strike Lightning - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/strike_lightning#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:strike_lightning` reward type summons a lightning bolt at a target location.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/strike_lightning#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `position` | `ArcPositionTarget` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Target position. |
| `visual_only` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `false` | Visual only (no damage). |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/strike_lightning#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example strikes lightning on an entity when the player hits it with a trident.

data/my\_datapack/arc/zeus\_trident.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_hurt_entity",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item_in_hand",            "item": "minecraft:trident"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:strike_lightning",            "position": "ENTITY",            "visual_only": false        }    ]}

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

---------\
\
The arc:health condition type checks the player's current health.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/health)

[🛡️ Is Blocking\
---------------\
\
The arc:is\_blocking condition type checks if the player is currently using a shield.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_blocking)

[🛏️ Is Sleeping\
---------------\
\
The arc:is\_sleeping condition type checks if the player is currently sleeping in a bed.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/is_sleeping)

[🔥 On Fire\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_fire condition type checks if the player is currently on fire.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/on_fire)

[🥩 Saturation Level\
-------------------\
\
.---](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/saturation_level)

# Hand - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:hand` condition type checks which hand was used to perform an interaction.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `hand` | `InteractionHand` | ✅Required | \-  | MAIN\_HAND or OFF\_HAND. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example only allows a specific interaction if done with the off-hand.

data/my\_datapack/arc/offhand\_interact.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_interact_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:hand",            "hand": "OFF_HAND"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:command",            "command": "say You used your off-hand!"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🖱️ On Interact Block\
---------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractblock action type is triggered when a player interacts with a block, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_interact_block)

[🖱️ On Interact Entity\
----------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractentity action type is triggered when a player interacts with an entity, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_interact_entity)

[🪵 On Strip Log\
---------------\
\
The arc:onstriplog action type is triggered when a player strips a log or wood block using an axe.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_strip_log)

[🖱️ On Use Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:onuseitem action type is triggered when a player uses an item (right-clicks with an item).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_use_item)

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#example)
    
*   [Compatible Action Types](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/players/hand#compatible-action-types)

---

# Item In Hand - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_in_hand#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:item_in_hand` condition type checks if the player is holding a specific item in their main hand, off-hand, or either hand.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_in_hand#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | [`ItemStack`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#item) | ✅Required | \-  | The item to check. |
| `hand` | [`InteractionHand`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#hand) | ❌Optional | `null` | The hand to check. Checks both hands if not set. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_in_hand#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example increases the player's destroy speed when they are breaking stone and holding a diamond pickaxe in their main hand.

data/my\_datapack/arc/fast\_stone\_mining.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_get_destroy_speed",    "conditions": [        { "type": "arc:block", "block": "minecraft:stone" },        {            "type": "arc:item_in_hand",            "hand": "MAIN_HAND",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond_pickaxe" }        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:destroy_speed_multiplier",            "multiplier": 2.0        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_in_hand#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🏆 On Advancement\
-----------------\
\
The arc:on\_advancement action type is triggered when a player receives an advancement.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/advancements/on_advancement)

------------------------\
\
The arc:onblockwith\_shield action type is triggered when a player successfully blocks damage using a shield.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_block_with_shield)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onbreakblock action type is triggered when a player breaks a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_break_block)

------------------\
\
The arc:onbreedanimal action type is triggered when a player breeds two animals.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_breed_animal)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onbrewpotion action type is triggered when a player brews a potion.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_brew_potion)

----------------------\
\
The arc:onchangedimension action type is triggered when a player travels between dimensions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_change_dimension)

------------\
\
The arc:on\_crouch action type is triggered when a player crouches (sneaks).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_crouch)

-----------\
\
The arc:on\_death action type is triggered when the player dies.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_death)

-----------\
\
The arc:on\_drink action type is triggered when a player drinks an item (e.g., potion, milk bucket, etc.).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_drink)

[🪣 On Drop Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:ondropitem action type is triggered when a player drops an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_drop_item)

----------\
\
The arc:on\_eat action type is triggered when a player eats an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_eat)

------------------\

The arc:onaddeffect action type is triggered when a player receives a potion effect.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_add_effect)

------------------\
\
The arc:onemptybucket action type is triggered when a player places a liquid or fish from a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_empty_bucket)

-----------------\
\
The arc:onenchantitem action type is triggered when a player enchants an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_enchant_item)

\
The arc:onfillbucket action type is triggered when a player picks up a liquid or fish with a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_fill_bucket)

--------------------\
\
The arc:onfishedup\_item action type is triggered when a player fishes up an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_fished_up_item)

----------------------\
\
The arc:getattackspeed action type is used to retrieve the attack speed of a player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_attack_speed)

----------------------\
\

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Effect Duration Multiplier - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:effect_duration_multiplier` reward type multiplies the duration of an effect a player receives.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `multiplier` | `double` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The multiplier for the effect's duration. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will double the duration of any beneficial effect the player receives.

data/my\_datapack/arc/extend\_buffs.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_add_effect",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:effect_category",            "category": "BENEFICIAL"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect_duration_multiplier",            "multiplier": 2.0        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/effects/effect_duration_multiplier#example)

---

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:repair_all_armor` reward type repairs all equipped armor items.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/repair_all_armor#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `amount` | `int` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Amount to repair. |
| `is_percentage` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `false` | Is amount a percentage. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/items/repair_all_armor#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example repairs armor by 10 durability when gaining XP.

data/my\_datapack/arc/mending\_lite.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_pickup_item",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:entity_type",            "entity_type": "minecraft:experience_orb"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:repair_all_armor",            "amount": 10        }    ]}

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Change Weather - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_weather#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:change_weather` reward type sets the current weather in the world.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_weather#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `weather` | `ArcWeatherType` | ✅Required | \-No default value | CLEAR, RAIN, or THUNDER. |
| `duration` | `int` | ❌Optional | `6000` | Duration in ticks. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_weather#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example makes it thunder when a player dies.

data/my\_datapack/arc/dramatic\_death.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_death",    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:change_weather",            "weather": "THUNDER",            "duration": 6000        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/world/change_weather#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

# Attack Speed Multiplier - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/attack_speed_multiplier#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:attack_speed_multiplier` reward type modifies the player's attack speed.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/attack_speed_multiplier#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `multiplier` | `float` | ✅Required | \-No default value | The multiplier for the attack speed. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/attack_speed_multiplier#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example will double the player's attack speed when they are holding a wooden sword.

data/my\_datapack/arc/fast\_wood\_sword.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_get_attack_speed",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item_in_hand",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:wooden_sword"            }        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:attack_speed_multiplier",            "multiplier": 2.0        }    ]}## Compatible Action Types<CompatibleActionTypes />

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/attack_speed_multiplier#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/attack_speed_multiplier#example)

---

# Heal - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/heal#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:heal` reward type restores health points to the player.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/heal#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `amount` | `float` | ✅Required | \-No default value | Amount to heal. |
| `is_percentage` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `false` | Is amount a percentage. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/heal#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example heals the player for 2 hearts (4 points) when killing a mob.

data/my\_datapack/arc/lifesteal.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_kill_entity",    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:heal",            "amount": 4.0        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/players/heal#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

# Light Level - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/light_level#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:light_level` condition type checks the light level at the player's position.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/light_level#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `light_level` | `int` | ✅Required | \-  | The light level. |
| `comparison` | `ComparisonType` | ❌Optional | `==` | Comparison type. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/light_level#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives night vision if the light level is below 4.

data/my\_datapack/arc/auto\_night\_vision.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_walk",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:light_level",            "light_level": 4,            "comparison": "<"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:night_vision",                "duration": 200            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/light_level#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

The arc:oninteractentity action type is triggered when a player interacts with an entity, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_interact_entity)

[💔 On Item Break\
----------------\
\
The arc:onitembreak action type is triggered when a tool or armor piece loses all durability and breaks.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_item_break)

[🦘 On Jump\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_jump action type is triggered when a player jumps.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_jump)

[💀 On Kill Entity\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onkillentity action type is triggered when a player kills an entity.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_kill_entity)

[⬇️ On Land\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_land action type is triggered when a player lands on the ground after falling or jumping.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_land)

[🤲 On Pickup Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onpickupitem action type is triggered when a player picks up an item from the ground.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_pickup_item)

[🧱 On Place Block\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onplaceblock action type is triggered when a player places a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_place_block)

[🌱 On Plant Crop\
----------------\
\
The arc:onplantcrop action type is triggered when a player plants a crop.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_plant_crop)

[🎣 On Rod Reel In\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onrodreel\_in action type is triggered when a player reels in a fishing rod.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_rod_reel_in)

[🏹 On Shoot Projectile\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

[🏃‍♂️ On Sprint\
---------------\
\
The arc:on\_sprint action type is triggered when a player is sprinting.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_sprint)

---------------------\

-------------------------\

The arc:onelytrafly\_start action type is triggered when a player starts flying with an elytra.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_elytra_fly_start)

\

---------------------\
\
The arc:onwalkstop action type is triggered when a player stops walking.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_walk_stop)

---------------\
\
The arc:onstriplog action type is triggered when a player strips a log or wood block using an axe.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_strip_log)

---------------\
\
The arc:ontillsoil action type is triggered when a player uses a hoe on a dirt-like block to create farmland.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_till_soil)

-------------------------\
\
The arc:ontradewith\_villager action type is triggered when a player successfully completes a trade with a Villager or Wandering Trader.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_trade_with_villager)

# Disarm Entity - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/disarm_entity#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:disarm_entity` reward type causes the target entity to drop an item from a specific slot.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/disarm_entity#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item_target` | `ArcItemTarget` | ❌Optional | `MAIN_HAND` | Which slot to disarm. |
| `position_target` | `ArcPositionTarget` | ❌Optional | `ENTITY` | Where to drop the item. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/disarm_entity#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example disarms the opponent's main hand when dealing a critical hit.

data/my\_datapack/arc/crit\_disarm.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_hurt_entity",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:critical_hit"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:disarm_entity",            "item_target": "MAIN_HAND"        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/reward_types/entities/disarm_entity#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Team - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/teams/team#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:team` condition type checks if a player is on a specific scoreboard team.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/teams/team#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `team` | `string` | ✅Required | \-  | The team name to check. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/teams/team#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives players on the "blue" team a blue wool when they break a block.

data/my\_datapack/arc/blue\_team\_reward.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_break_block",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:team",            "team": "blue"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:blue_wool" }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/teams/team#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-----------------------\
\
The arc:onsprintstop action type is triggered when a player stops sprinting.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_sprint_stop)

[🏊‍♂️ On Stop Swimming\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onswimstop action type is triggered when a player stops swimming.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_swim_stop)

---------------------\
\
The arc:onwalkstop action type is triggered when a player stops walking.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_walk_stop)

---------------\
\
The arc:onstriplog action type is triggered when a player strips a log or wood block using an axe.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_strip_log)

-------------\
\
The arc:on\_swim action type is triggered when a player is swimming.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_swim)

-----------------\
\
The arc:ontameanimal action type is triggered when a player tames an animal.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_tame_animal)

\

---------------\

\

-------------\
\
