Learn More

On this page

The `arc:item` condition type checks if an item matches a specific item, including optional checks for count and components.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | [`ItemStack`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#item) | ✅Required | \-  | The item to check. |
| `check_components` | `boolean` | ❌Optional | `true` | Whether to check item components. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#example "Direct link to Example")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives the player a speed boost when they drink a Potion of Swiftness.

data/my\_datapack/arc/boost\_swiftness\_potion.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_drink",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item",            "item": {                "id": "minecraft:potion",                "components": {                    "minecraft:potion_contents": {                        "potion": "minecraft:swiftness"                    }                }            }        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect_amplifier_addition",            "addition": 1        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[🧪 On Brew Potion\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onbrewpotion action type is triggered when a player brews a potion.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_brew_potion)

[🛠️ On Craft Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:oncraftitem action type is triggered when a player crafts an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_craft_item)

[🥤 On Drink\
-----------\
\
The arc:on\_drink action type is triggered when a player drinks an item (e.g., potion, milk bucket, etc.).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_drink)

[🪣 On Drop Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:ondropitem action type is triggered when a player drops an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_drop_item)

[🍽️ On Eat\
----------\
\
The arc:on\_eat action type is triggered when a player eats an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_eat)

[🪣 On Empty Bucket\
------------------\
\
The arc:onemptybucket action type is triggered when a player places a liquid or fish from a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_empty_bucket)

[✨ On Enchant Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onenchantitem action type is triggered when a player enchants an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_enchant_item)

[💧 On Fill Bucket\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onfillbucket action type is triggered when a player picks up a liquid or fish with a bucket.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_fill_bucket)

[🎣 On Fished Up Item\
--------------------\
\
The arc:onfishedup\_item action type is triggered when a player fishes up an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_fished_up_item)

[⚔️ On Get Attack Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:getattackspeed action type is used to retrieve the attack speed of a player.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_get_attack_speed)

[⚡ On Get Destroy Speed\
----------------------\
\
The arc:ongetdestroy\_speed action type is triggered when a player is determining how quickly they can break a block. This action type allows you to modify the destroy speed based on various conditions.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_get_destroy_speed)

[⚙️ On Grind Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:ongrinditem action type is triggered when a player grinds an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_grind_item)

[🗡️ On Hurt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onhurtitem action type is triggered when a player hurts/damages an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_hurt_item)

[🖱️ On Interact Block\
---------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractblock action type is triggered when a player interacts with a block, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_interact_block)

[🖱️ On Interact Entity\
----------------------\
\
The arc:oninteractentity action type is triggered when a player interacts with an entity, such as right-clicking it.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_interact_entity)

[💔 On Item Break\
----------------\
\
The arc:onitembreak action type is triggered when a tool or armor piece loses all durability and breaks.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_item_break)

[🤲 On Pickup Item\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onpickupitem action type is triggered when a player picks up an item from the ground.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_pickup_item)

[🏹 On Shoot Projectile\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

[🪵 On Strip Log\
---------------\
\
The arc:onstriplog action type is triggered when a player strips a log or wood block using an axe.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_strip_log)

[🏹 On Throw Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onthrowitem action type is triggered when a player throws an item (e.g. snowball, egg, ender pearl).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_throw_item)

[🌿 On Till Soil\
---------------\
\
The arc:ontillsoil action type is triggered when a player uses a hoe on a dirt-like block to create farmland.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_till_soil)

[🤝 On Trade With Villager\
-------------------------\
\
The arc:ontradewith\_villager action type is triggered when a player successfully completes a trade with a Villager or Wandering Trader.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/entities/on_trade_with_villager)

[⚒️ On Use Anvil\
---------------\
\
The arc:onuseanvil action type is triggered when a player uses an anvil to repair or rename an item.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_use_anvil)

[🖱️ On Use Item\
---------------\
\
The arc:onuseitem action type is triggered when a player uses an item (right-clicks with an item).](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_use_item)

*   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#parameters)
    
*   [Example](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#example)
    
*   [Compatible Action Types](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item#compatible-action-types)

# Is Wet - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_wet#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:is_wet` condition type checks if the player is wet (in water or rain).

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_wet#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This condition type does not have any parameters.

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_wet#example "Direct link to Example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example increases damage taken from lightning if the player is wet.

data/my\_datapack/arc/wet\_lightning.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_get_hurt",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:damage_source",            "source": "lightningBolt"        },        {            "type": "arc:is_wet"        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:damage_multiplier",            "multiplier": 1.5        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/movement/is_wet#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

# Coins - Jobs+ | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/coins#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

Coins in Jobs+ are used to buy power-ups and more jobs. To earn them, you must level up in your job. Every time you level up, you gain 1 coin. If you wish, this can be changed in the config. If you want to know how many coins you have, open the Jobs+ GUI and the coin amount will be shown at the top.

# Jobs+ | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

![Jobs+](https://daqem.com/img/projects/jobsplus.webp)

Jobs+
=====

Adds 10 jobs to Minecraft. You can now add your own jobs!

[](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/jobs-remastered)
[](https://modrinth.com/mod/jobs-remastered)
[](https://github.com/DAQEM/JobsPlusRemastered)

Introduction[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus#introduction "Direct link to Introduction")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Jobs+ is a mod that adds 10 jobs to Minecraft. These jobs are **Alchemist**, **Builder**, **Digger**, **Enchanter**, **Farmer**, **Fisherman**, **Hunter**, **Lumberjack**, **Miner** and **Smith**. Each job has unique power-ups which you can unlock with coins which you can get by progressing through your job.

You are not limited to these 10 default jobs, with this mod, you can make your own custom jobs, recipes and power-ups! This way you can make your own unique experiences and there are endless possibilities.

*   [Introduction](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus#introduction)

---

# Item Equipped - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_equipped#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:item_equipped` condition type checks if the player has a specific item equipped in any armor slot.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_equipped#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `item` | [`ItemStack`](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/json_formats/#item) | ✅Required | \-  | The item to check. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/items/item_equipped#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives the player Resistance when they take damage while wearing a full set of diamond armor.

data/my\_datapack/arc/diamond\_armor\_resistance.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_get_hurt",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:item_equipped",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond_helmet" }        },        {            "type": "arc:item_equipped",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond_chestplate" }        },        {            "type": "arc:item_equipped",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond_leggings" }        },        {            "type": "arc:item_equipped",            "item": { "id": "minecraft:diamond_boots" }        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": "minecraft:resistance",            "duration": 100,            "amplifier": 0        }    ]}

\

-----------------\
\
The arc:onpickupitem action type is triggered when a player picks up an item from the ground.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_pickup_item)

[🧱 On Place Block\
-----------------\
\
The arc:onplaceblock action type is triggered when a player places a block.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/blocks/on_place_block)

[🏹 On Shoot Projectile\
----------------------\
\
The arc:onshootprojectile action type is triggered when a player shoots a projectile, such as an arrow or a snowball.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/players/on_shoot_projectile)

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

---------------\
\
The arc:on\_sprint action type is triggered when a player is sprinting.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/movement/on_sprint)

# Time of Day - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/time_of_day#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:time_of_day` condition type checks if the world time is within a specified range (0-24000).

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/time_of_day#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `min_time` | `int` | ✅Required | \-  | Minimum time (0-24000). |
| `max_time` | `int` | ✅Required | \-  | Maximum time (0-24000). |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/time_of_day#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives strength during the night (13000-23000).

data/my\_datapack/arc/night\_strength.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_walk",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:time_of_day",            "min_time": 13000,            "max_time": 23000        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:strength",                "duration": 40            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/time_of_day#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

# Y Level - Arc Lib | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/y_level#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `arc:y_level` condition type checks if the player's vertical position is within a specified range.

Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/y_level#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `min_y` | `int` | ❌Optional | `-2147483648` | Minimum Y level. |
| `max_y` | `int` | ❌Optional | `2147483647` | Maximum Y level. |

Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/y_level#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following example gives mining fatigue when below Y level 0.

data/my\_datapack/arc/deep\_fatigue.json

    {    "holder": {        "type": "arc:player",        "id": "arc:player"    },    "type": "arc:on_walk",    "conditions": [        {            "type": "arc:y_level",            "max_y": 0        }    ],    "rewards": [        {            "type": "arc:effect",            "effect": {                "id": "minecraft:mining_fatigue",                "duration": 40            }        }    ]}

Compatible Action Types[​](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/condition_types/world/y_level#compatible-action-types "Direct link to Compatible Action Types")

[🔥 On Smelt Item\
----------------\
\
The arc:onsmeltitem action type is triggered when a player takes an item out of a furnace, smoker, or blast furnace. Just like experience orbs in vanilla Minecraft, this action also counts the items that are taken out with a hopper. As long as the player takes out one of the smelted items, the action will be triggered. The action is called for every item smelted individually, regardless of how many are taken out at once.](https://daqem.com/projects/arc/wiki/action_types/items/on_smelt_item)

The following pages will help you get started with Jobs+:

[🚀 How to Start\
---------------\
\
To start your Jobs journey, follow the instructions listed below:](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_start)

[🎯 How to Get Experience\
------------------------\
\
Listed below are the ways you can earn Job EXP for the 10 jobs that are included in Jobs+:](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp)

[💰 Coins\
--------\
\
Coins in Jobs+ are used to buy power-ups and more jobs. To earn them, you must level up in your job. Every time you level up, you gain 1 coin. If you wish, this can be changed in the config. If you want to know how many coins you have, open the Jobs+ GUI and the coin amount will be shown at the top.](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/coins)

[📝 Commands\
-----------\
\
There are many commands that will help you progress in your jobs experience the way you want to. Please note that you need OP to use these commands. The commands can be found below.](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/commands)

To start your Jobs journey, follow the instructions listed below:

1.  Press J (this will open the Jobs+ menu)
2.  Select a job on the left hand side of the menu and click start performing this job. You can do this twice as you get 2 free jobs to begin with. After this, each job costs 10 coins.

Once you have started a job, you can start earning job exp by doing job tasks.

---

# Commands - Jobs+ | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/commands#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

There are many commands that will help you progress in your jobs experience the way you want to. Please note that you need OP to use these commands. The commands can be found below.

**/job set coins \[playername\] \[number of coins\]** sets the number of coins for a player.

**/job set experience \[playername\] \[job\] \[amount of experience\]** sets the experience for the players job.

**/job set level \[playername\] \[job\] \[level\]** sets the job level for the player.

**/job set powerup \[playername\] \[job\] clear** clears all power-ups for the specified job.

**/job set powerup \[playername\] \[job\] ACTIVE** makes the powerup active.

**/job set powerup \[playername\] \[job\] INACTIVE** deactivates the powerup.

**/job set powerup \[playername\] \[job\] LOCKED** means you don't have the required level or coins. It can also mean you need to buy the power-up before it first.

**/job set powerup \[playername\] \[job\] NOT-OWNED** means you don't own the power-up, but it is available to buy.

# How to get Job EXP - Jobs+ | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Listed below are the ways you can earn Job EXP for the 10 jobs that are included in Jobs+:

#### Alchemist:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#alchemist "Direct link to Alchemist:")

*   Killing alchemy mobs (phantoms, pufferfish, magma cubes, ghasts, rabbits, spiders, caves spiders, blazes and witches)
*   Brewing potions
*   Crafting potion ingredients (brewing stands, glistering melon slices, sugar, blaze powder, fermented spider eyes, magma cream, golden carrots and turtle shells)
*   Throwing potions
*   Drinking potions
*   Harvesting Nether Wart

#### Builder:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#builder "Direct link to Builder:")

*   Placing blocks

#### Digger:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#digger "Direct link to Digger:")

*   Digging blocks

#### Enchanter:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#enchanter "Direct link to Enchanter:")

*   Enchanting items and books

#### Farmer:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#farmer "Direct link to Farmer:")

*   Planting crops
*   Harvesting crops
*   Taming animals
*   Breeding animals

#### Fisherman:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#fisherman "Direct link to Fisherman:")

*   Fishing
*   Swimming

#### Hunter:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#hunter "Direct link to Hunter:")

*   Cooking meat
*   Killing mobs

#### Lumberjack:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#lumberjack "Direct link to Lumberjack:")

*   Breaking wooden blocks
*   Stripping logs

#### Miner:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#miner "Direct link to Miner:")

*   Mining ores and stone type blocks

#### Smith:[​](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#smith "Direct link to Smith:")

*   Using a grindstone
*   Smelting ores
*   Crafting tools
*   Crafting armor
*   Using an anvil

*   [Alchemist:](https://daqem.com/projects/jobsplus/wiki/getting-started/how_to_get_exp#alchemist)

| Harmful Potion Immunity V | Become completely immune to harmful potions. | Alchemist level 50 | 18 job coins |

| Longer Potions II | Potions last 40% longer. | Alchemist level 10 | 10 job coins |
| Longer Potions III | Potions last 60% longer. | Alchemist level 20 | 10 job coins |

# YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

![YAML Config](https://daqem.com/img/projects/yaml-config.webp)

YAML Config
===========

Config library with GUI based on the YAML format.

[](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/yaml-config)
[](https://modrinth.com/mod/yaml-config)
[](https://github.com/DAQEM/YAMLConfig)

**YAML Config** is a powerful, multi-format configuration library for Minecraft mods. It provides a robust backend for handling configuration files and a built-in, user-friendly in-game GUI for editing them.

Designed for both **Fabric** and **NeoForge**, YAML Config abstracts away the complexity of file I/O and networking, allowing developers to focus on adding features to their mods.

### Key Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

*   **Multi-Format Support:** Use YAML, JSON5, TOML, or HOCON for your configuration files.
*   **In-Game GUI:** Press **F12** to open an automatically generated configuration screen. No extra coding required for UI.
*   **Automatic Syncing:** Server-side configurations are automatically synced to clients upon joining.
*   **Rich Entry Types:** Supports everything from basic booleans and integers to complex Lists, Maps, Enums, and Minecraft Registries.
*   **Validation:** Built-in support for minimum/maximum values, regex patterns, and validity checks.
*   **Hot-Reloading:** Configs can be reloaded on the fly (depending on specific implementation).

*   [Key Features](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config#key-features)

---

# Maven Repository | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

DAQEM Studios hosts a public Maven repository for developers to depend on our libraries and mods.

2026 Versioning Strategy

Only mods and libraries that follow our [2026 Versioning Strategy](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning)
 are hosted on this Maven. Legacy versions are not available on our Maven Repository. For legacy versions, use [CurseMaven](https://www.cursemaven.com/)
 or [Modrinth Maven](https://support.modrinth.com/en/articles/8801191-modrinth-maven)
.

Repository Information[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#repository-information "Direct link to Repository Information")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We provide two repositories based on the stability of the build.

| Type | URL | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Releases** | `https://maven.daqem.com/releases` | Stable, official releases only. |
| **Snapshots** | `https://maven.daqem.com/snapshots` | Development versions, Pull Requests, and pre-releases. |

* * *

Adding the Repository[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#adding-the-repository "Direct link to Adding the Repository")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add the following to your `build.gradle` or `settings.gradle` file:

### Groovy[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#groovy "Direct link to Groovy")

    repositories {    maven {        name = "DAQEM Maven"        url = "https://maven.daqem.com/releases"    }}

### Kotlin[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#kotlin "Direct link to Kotlin")

    repositories {    maven {        name = "DAQEM Maven"        url = uri("https://maven.daqem.com/releases")    }}

* * *

Available Artifacts[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#available-artifacts "Direct link to Available Artifacts")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below are the Group IDs and Artifact IDs for our projects. Use the suffix `-fabric`, `-neoforge`, or `-common` depending on your target platform.

| Project | Group ID | Artifact ID Base |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Arc Lib** | `com.daqem.arc` | `arc` |
| **Grief Logger** | `com.daqem.grieflogger` | `grieflogger` |
| **Item Restrictions** | `com.daqem.itemrestrictions` | `itemrestrictions` |
| **Jobs+** | `com.daqem.jobsplus` | `jobsplus` |
| **Jobs+ Tools** | `com.daqem.jobsplustools` | `jobsplustools` |
| **Necessities** | `com.daqem.necessities` | `necessities` |
| **Tiny Mob Farm** | `com.daqem.tinymobfarm` | `tinymobfarm` |
| **UI Lib** | `com.daqem.uilib` | `uilib` |
| **YAML Config** | `com.daqem.yamlconfig` | `yamlconfig` |

* * *

Dependency Example[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#dependency-example "Direct link to Dependency Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In a Fabric project using Architectury Loom, your `build.gradle` would look like this:

    dependencies {    // Standard Mod Implementation    modImplementation "com.daqem.arc:arc-fabric:${arc_version}"    modImplementation "com.daqem.uilib:uilib-fabric:${uilib_version}"    modImplementation "com.daqem.jobsplus:jobsplus-fabric:${jobsplus_version}"    // For Multi-platform Common projects    // modImplementation "com.daqem.yamlconfig:yamlconfig-common:${yamlconfig_version}"}

Snapshot Versions[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#snapshot-versions "Direct link to Snapshot Versions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Snapshot versions should not be used in production environments. They are intended for development purposes only. We mainly use snapshots when porting to new Minecraft versions so we can publish all mods at the same time since a lot of them depend on each other. To use snapshot versions, change the repository URL to the snapshots URL:

    repositories {    maven {        name = "DAQEM Maven Snapshots"        url = "https://maven.daqem.com/snapshots"    }}

Then, use the version with `-PRx` suffix, e.g., `1.0.0-PR3`.

    dependencies {    modImplementation "com.daqem.arc:arc-fabric:1.0.0-PR3"}

*   [Repository Information](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#repository-information)
    
*   [Adding the Repository](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#adding-the-repository)
    *   [Groovy](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#groovy)
        
    *   [Kotlin](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#kotlin)
        
*   [Available Artifacts](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#available-artifacts)
    
*   [Dependency Example](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#dependency-example)
    
*   [Snapshot Versions](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/maven#snapshot-versions)

# Version Strategy | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

note

This versioning strategy is applied to all DAQEM Studios projects from **2026 onward**.

DAQEM Studios follows a structured **Major.Minor.Patch** versioning system. Our system is specifically designed to help players and server owners immediately identify Minecraft version compatibility.

How Versioning Works[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#how-versioning-works "Direct link to How Versioning Works")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1\. Major Version (Minecraft Version Compatibility)[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#1-major-version-minecraft-version-compatibility "Direct link to 1. Major Version (Minecraft Version Compatibility)")

To simplify compatibility, our **Major version** starts at 1 for Minecraft 1.20 and increments by 1 for each new Minecraft Update/Game Drop. This means that if you see a mod version `19.x.x`, you can immediately know it is compatible with Minecraft 1.21.11, regardless of the minor or patch numbers.

| Minecraft Version | Major Version |
| --- | --- |
| **1.20** | `1.x.x` |
| **1.21** | `8.x.x` |
| **1.21.4** | `12.x.x` |
| **1.21.11** | `19.x.x` |

### 2\. Minor Version (Feature Parity)[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#2-minor-version-feature-parity "Direct link to 2. Minor Version (Feature Parity)")

The **Minor version** tracks new features and content.

If we add a new feature to the latest Game Drop and decide to "down-port" that feature to older Minecraft versions, the Minor version will increase across **all** supported versions. This ensures that if you see `13.2.0` and `19.2.0`, you know they contain the exact same features, despite being on different Minecraft versions.

### 3\. Patch Version (Local Bug Fixes)[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#3-patch-version-local-bug-fixes "Direct link to 3. Patch Version (Local Bug Fixes)")

The **Patch version** is for bug fixes. Unlike minor versions, patches are usually specific to a single Minecraft version. If a bug only exists on 1.21.1, we will update the patch number for that version only.

* * *

Maintenance & Down-porting Policy[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#maintenance--down-porting-policy "Direct link to Maintenance & Down-porting Policy")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our goal is to keep our mods available on the newest versions of Minecraft as quickly as possible. However, we do not provide equal long-term support for every version.

### Game Drop Updates[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#game-drop-updates "Direct link to Game Drop Updates")

When a new Minecraft Game Drop is released, we will update the mod to support it as soon as possible. This ensures the mod is always playable on the "bleeding edge" of Minecraft.

### Feature Support (Minor Updates)[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#feature-support-minor-updates "Direct link to Feature Support (Minor Updates)")

While we update for every Game Drop, we only **down-port** new features (Minor version updates) to the **Latest Release** and the **most popular/widely-used Minecraft versions**.

*   **Supported for Features:** The latest Game Drop (e.g., 1.21.11) and "Long-term" community favorites (e.g., 1.20.1, 1.21.1).
*   **Unsupported for Features:** Intermediate versions (e.g., 1.20.4, 1.21.2, 1.21.6). While the mod may be released for these versions so you can play it, they will likely never receive "Minor" version updates or new features after the initial release because they are less commonly used and would require extra maintenance effort.

**Example Scenario:**

*   **1.21.11 (Latest):** Receives the mod, all bug fixes, and all new features.
*   **1.21.1 (Popular):** Receives bug fixes and new features ported from the latest version.
*   **1.21.6 (Intermediate):** Receives the initial mod release and critical bug fixes, but **no new features** (Minor version remains at `.0`).

* * *

Summary Table[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#summary-table "Direct link to Summary Table")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Change Type | Version Component | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **New Minecraft Game Drop** | Major | `18.x.x` → `19.x.x` |
| **New Feature / Down-port** | Minor | `19.0.x` → `19.1.0` |
| **Version Specific Bug Fix** | Patch | `13.0.0` → `13.0.1` |

Version Mapping Table[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#version-mapping-table "Direct link to Version Mapping Table")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use this table to identify which DAQEM Studios **Major Version** corresponds to your Minecraft version.

| Minecraft Version | Major Version | Support Level¹ |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **1.20** | `1` | **Popular** (Features & Fixes) |
| **1.20.1** | `2` | **Popular** (Features & Fixes) |
| **1.20.2** | `3` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.20.3** | `4` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.20.4** | `5` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.20.5** | `6` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.20.6** | `7` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21** | `8` | **Popular** (Features & Fixes) |
| **1.21.1** | `9` | **Popular** (Features & Fixes) |
| **1.21.2** | `10` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.3** | `11` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.4** | `12` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.5** | `13` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.6** | `14` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.7** | `15` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.8** | `16` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.9** | `17` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.10** | `18` | Intermediate (Fixes only) |
| **1.21.11** | `19` | **Latest** (Full Support) |

*   [How Versioning Works](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#how-versioning-works)
    *   [1\. Major Version (Minecraft Version Compatibility)](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#1-major-version-minecraft-version-compatibility)
        
    *   [2\. Minor Version (Feature Parity)](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#2-minor-version-feature-parity)
        
    *   [3\. Patch Version (Local Bug Fixes)](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#3-patch-version-local-bug-fixes)
        
*   [Maintenance & Down-porting Policy](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#maintenance--down-porting-policy)
    *   [Game Drop Updates](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#game-drop-updates)
        
    *   [Feature Support (Minor Updates)](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#feature-support-minor-updates)
        
*   [Summary Table](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#summary-table)
    
*   [Version Mapping Table](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/versioning#version-mapping-table)

# Platforms | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

At DAQEM Studios, we strive to make our mods accessible to as many players as possible. To achieve this, we support the major mod loaders available in the Minecraft ecosystem. However, due to the evolving nature of the modding community and technical changes within the loaders themselves, our platform support varies depending on the Minecraft version.

Supported Platforms Strategy[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#supported-platforms-strategy "Direct link to Supported Platforms Strategy")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our support strategy helps us maintain stability while embracing the future of Minecraft modding. We adhere to the following strict platform split:

| Minecraft Version | Supported Mod Loaders |
| --- | --- |
| **1.21.1 and Higher** | ✅ **NeoForge** & ✅ **Fabric** |
| **1.20.1 and Lower** | ✅ **Forge** & ✅ **Fabric** |

* * *

The Role of Architectury API[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#the-role-of-architectury-api "Direct link to The Role of Architectury API")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To maintain high-quality mods across multiple platforms simultaneously without slowing down development, we utilize **Architectury API**.

### What is Architectury?[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#what-is-architectury "Direct link to What is Architectury?")

Architectury API is an intermediate library that acts as a bridge between our mod code and the specific mod loader (Forge, Fabric, or NeoForge). Instead of writing the same code three times for three different platforms, we write our logic once using Architectury's events and hooks. The API then translates that logic into instructions the specific mod loader understands.

### Why do we use it?[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#why-do-we-use-it "Direct link to Why do we use it?")

1.  **Faster Updates:** When a new Minecraft version is released, we can update our mods significantly faster because the core logic often remains compatible.
2.  **Unified Codebase:** Bugs are fixed in one place and applied to all platforms instantly. This reduces platform-specific bugs.
3.  **Feature Parity:** Users on Fabric get the exact same features as users on NeoForge/Forge, ensuring a consistent experience regardless of your loader preference.

Dependency Required

Because our mods rely on this abstraction layer, **Architectury API is a required dependency** for almost all DAQEM Studios mods. You must install the version of Architectury API matching your Minecraft version and Mod Loader.

* * *

Platform Details[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#platform-details "Direct link to Platform Details")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Fabric[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#fabric "Direct link to Fabric")

**Supported Versions:** All

**Fabric** is a lightweight, modular mod loader known for its speed and efficient update cycle. We support Fabric across all maintained versions of our mods (both 1.20.1- and 1.21+).

*   **Requirements:** You will typically need the **Fabric Loader** and the **Fabric API** mod installed alongside **Architectury API** and our projects.

### NeoForge[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#neoforge "Direct link to NeoForge")

**Supported Versions:** 1.21.1+

**NeoForge** is a fork of Forge created by nearly the entire original Forge team. It represents the modern standard for the "Forge-style" modding ecosystem. Starting with Minecraft 1.21, DAQEM Studios has migrated exclusively to NeoForge for our heavier mod loader support.

*   **Why the switch?** NeoForge offers a cleaner development environment and a robust community structure that aligns with our goals for long-term maintenance.
*   **Compatibility:** NeoForge is **not** compatible with standard Forge.

### Forge[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#forge "Direct link to Forge")

**Supported Versions:** 1.20.1 and older

**Minecraft Forge** was the standard mod loader for many years. We continue to support Forge for our "Legacy" versions (1.20.1, 1.19.x, etc.).

*   **Future Support:** We do not plan to develop new updates for Forge on Minecraft versions 1.21 or higher. Our focus for those versions is strictly NeoForge.

* * *

Frequently Asked Questions[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#frequently-asked-questions "Direct link to Frequently Asked Questions")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Can I use your 1.21 mod on standard Forge?[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#can-i-use-your-121-mod-on-standard-forge "Direct link to Can I use your 1.21 mod on standard Forge?")

No. For Minecraft 1.21 and above, we only release files for **NeoForge** and **Fabric**. The internal architecture differences between Forge and NeoForge in modern versions are significant enough that we cannot support both simultaneously.

### Does "Fabric" support include Quilt?[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#does-fabric-support-include-quilt "Direct link to Does "Fabric" support include Quilt?")

While we do not officially test against **Quilt**, most of our Fabric mods should work on Quilt due to its backward compatibility with Fabric mods. However, if you encounter a crash specific to Quilt, we may not be able to offer support.

### Where can I find the correct file?[​](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#where-can-i-find-the-correct-file "Direct link to Where can I find the correct file?")

When downloading our mods from CurseForge or Modrinth, pay close attention to the **Game Version** and the **Mod Loader** tag.

*   For **1.21+**: Look for the NeoForge or Fabric tag.
*   For **1.20.1-**: Look for the Forge or Fabric tag.

*   [Supported Platforms Strategy](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#supported-platforms-strategy)
    
*   [The Role of Architectury API](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#the-role-of-architectury-api)
    *   [What is Architectury?](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#what-is-architectury)
        
    *   [Why do we use it?](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#why-do-we-use-it)
        
*   [Platform Details](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#platform-details)
    *   [Fabric](https://daqem.com/docs/daqem/wiki/platforms#fabric)

# X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

👋Welcome!

Welcome to the official wiki for the **X-Life Mod**, a high-stakes survival challenge for Minecraft. This wiki contains everything you need to know to install, configure, and survive in the world of X-Life.

What is X-Life? The Backstory[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life#what-is-x-life-the-backstory "Direct link to What is X-Life? The Backstory")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

X-Life was a popular, modded Minecraft SMP (Survival Multiplayer) series that began in 2020, featuring a large group of YouTubers. The series was built around a unique and challenging core mechanic: players had a limited number of lives, and their health was directly tied to how many lives they had left.

The rules of the original series were as follows:

*   **Ten Lives Total:** Each player had a total of 10 lives.
*   **Start with One Heart:** On their first life, players started with only a single heart.
*   **Gain a Heart on Death:** Every time a player died, they would respawn with one additional heart, making survival a little easier, but bringing them one step closer to elimination.
*   **Final Elimination:** After losing the tenth and final life, the player would be banned from the server permanently.

This simple but punishing ruleset created a tense and exciting environment where every decision mattered, leading to cautious exploration, strategic alliances, and memorable moments. This mod aims to replicate that thrilling experience for everyone.

* * *

Core Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life#core-features "Direct link to Core Features")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The X-Life Mod brings the challenging spirit of the SMP series to your own server.

### ❤️ Lives & Health System

The core mechanic: start with 10 lives, lose health with each death, and face a permanent ban on your final life. Your heart color changes to reflect your current life count.

### ✨ Extra Life Item

A craftable item that allows you to regain a lost life, giving you a fighting chance to survive.

### ⚙️ Server Configuration

Customize the experience by inverting heart colors or allowing enchanted golden apples to grant extra lives.

### 🔧 Admin Commands

Server operators can easily manage player lives with simple, in-game commands.

* * *

Where to Start[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life#where-to-start "Direct link to Where to Start")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ready to take on the challenge?

1.  **[🚀 Installation](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation)
    :** A step-by-step guide to adding the X-Life mod and its dependencies to your server.
2.  **[⚙️ Configuration](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration)
    :** Learn how to tweak the settings to customize your experience.
3.  **[❤️ The Lives System](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system)
    :** Understand the core rules of health, death, and survival.

* * *

What is Necessities?

**Necessities** is a modern, cross-platform equivalent to the classic "Essentials" plugins found on Bukkit/Spigot servers. It provides the core commands and utilities that every server needs: teleportation, homes, kits, moderation tools, and more.

Key Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities#key-features "Direct link to Key Features")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Teleportation:** Robust `/tpa`, `/home`, `/warp`, and `/spawn` systems with configurable delays and cooldowns.
*   **Player Utilities:** Heal, feed, fly, god mode, and speed management.
*   **Inventory Management:** Access Ender Chests (`/enderchest`) and view other players' inventories (`/invsee`) remotely.
*   **Kits:** Create custom kits using JSON files to give players starter gear or rewards.
*   **Moderation:** Vanish mode, broadcasting, and nickname management.
*   **World Control:** Easily change time and weather with shorthand commands.
*   **Cross-Platform:** Works on both **Fabric** and **NeoForge** using the same codebase.

Integration[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities#integration "Direct link to Integration")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Necessities integrates seamlessly with:

*   **Fabric Permissions API** (Fabric) / **PermissionAPI** (NeoForge) for granular command control.
*   **YAML Config** for easy-to-read configuration files.

# Installation - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

**YAML Config** is a shared library. This means it is a dependency for other mods. If a mod requires YAML Config, you generally must install it, or the game will fail to launch.

### Prerequisites[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#prerequisites "Direct link to Prerequisites")

Before installing, ensure you have the following:

1.  **Minecraft Java Edition:** Version 1.21 or higher.
2.  **Java 21:** Modern Minecraft versions require Java 21.
3.  **A Mod Loader:** Either Fabric or NeoForge installed on your client/server.

### Required Dependencies[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#required-dependencies "Direct link to Required Dependencies")

YAML Config has **one mandatory dependency**:

*   **UI Lib**: This is the library DAQEM Studios uses to render the beautiful in-game menus. YAML Config _will not work_ without it.

### Installation Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps "Direct link to Installation Steps")

#### 1\. Download the Mods[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#1-download-the-mods "Direct link to 1. Download the Mods")

Go to your preferred mod hosting site (CurseForge or Modrinth) and download the following `.jar` files for your specific Minecraft version:

*   `yamlconfig-x.x.x.jar`
*   `uilib-x.x.x.jar`

#### 2\. Locate your Mods Folder[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#2-locate-your-mods-folder "Direct link to 2. Locate your Mods Folder")

*   **Windows:** Press `Win + R`, type `%appdata%/.minecraft/mods`, and press Enter.
*   **Mac:** In Finder, go to `Go > Go to Folder` and type `~/Library/Application Support/minecraft/mods`.
*   **Linux:** Go to `~/.minecraft/mods`.

#### 3\. Install[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#3-install "Direct link to 3. Install")

Drag and drop both the **YAML Config** and **UI Lib** jar files into the `mods` folder.

#### 4\. Platform Specifics[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#4-platform-specifics "Direct link to 4. Platform Specifics")

**For Fabric Users**

If you are using the Fabric Loader, you must also ensure you have the **Fabric API** installed. Most mods require this, and YAML Config utilizes it for networking and resource management.

1.  Download **Fabric API**.
2.  Place it in the `mods` folder alongside YAML Config.

**For NeoForge Users**

No additional API mods are strictly required for NeoForge, as the functionality is built-in. Just ensure you have the correct version of the NeoForge loader installed.

### Server Installation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#server-installation "Direct link to Server Installation")

If you are running a dedicated server, you **must install YAML Config and UI Lib on the server**.

*   **Why?** The mod handles networking synchronization. When a player joins, the server sends its configuration values to the client. If the mod is missing on the server, this synchronization cannot happen, and clients may have desynchronized gameplay (e.g., recipes working on the client but failing on the server).

### Troubleshooting[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#troubleshooting "Direct link to Troubleshooting")

**The game crashes on startup!**

*   Check the crash report. Does it say `java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/daqem/uilib/...`?
*   **Solution:** You forgot to install **UI Lib**.
*   Does it say `Mod 'examplemod' requires 'yamlconfig'`?
*   **Solution:** You forgot to install **YAML Config**.

**I can't see the config menu!**

*   Ensure both YAML Config and the mod you are trying to configure are installed correctly.
*   Check if there is a keybinding conflict (see [Accessing the Menu](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings)
    ).

*   [Prerequisites](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#prerequisites)
    
*   [Required Dependencies](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#required-dependencies)
    
*   [Installation Steps](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps)
    *   [1\. Download the Mods](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#1-download-the-mods)
        
    *   [2\. Locate your Mods Folder](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#2-locate-your-mods-folder)
        
    *   [3\. Install](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#3-install)
        
    *   [4\. Platform Specifics](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#4-platform-specifics)
        
*   [Server Installation](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#server-installation)
    
*   [Troubleshooting](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/installation#troubleshooting)

# Accessing the Menu - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Unlike older modding standards that required you to restart the game to change settings, YAML Config provides a unified, in-game dashboard to manage everything on the fly.

### The Default Keybinding[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#the-default-keybinding "Direct link to The Default Keybinding")

By default, the global configuration menu can be opened by pressing:

**F12**
=======

### Changing the Keybinding[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#changing-the-keybinding "Direct link to Changing the Keybinding")

If **F12** conflicts with another mod (or a system screenshot key), you can easily rebind it:

1.  Open the Minecraft **Pause Menu** (`Esc`).
2.  Go to **Options** > **Controls** > **Key Binds**.
3.  Scroll down to the **YAML Config** category.
4.  Click on **Open Configs Screen** and press your desired key.

### The Configs Screen[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#the-configs-screen "Direct link to The Configs Screen")

When you press the hotkey, you will be greeted by the **Configs Screen**. This screen acts as a hub for all installed mods that utilize the YAML Config library.

*   **Mod List:** You will see cards for every supported mod.
*   **Search/Navigation:** If you have many mods installed, they will be organized in a grid.
*   **Opening a Config:** Simply click the button corresponding to the mod name (e.g., "Jobs+", "GriefLogger") to enter its specific settings.

### Server-Side vs. Client-Side[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#server-side-vs-client-side "Direct link to Server-Side vs. Client-Side")

When you click a mod in the list:

1.  **Client Configs:** These open immediately. You are editing the file located on your own computer.
2.  **Common/Server Configs:**
    *   If you are in **Singleplayer**, it opens immediately.
    *   If you are in **Multiplayer**:
        *   **If you are OP (Operator):** The client requests the config from the server. The menu opens, allowing you to edit global server settings.
        *   **If you are NOT OP:** You will only be able to view client side configs. Common and server configs will be hidden, as you do not have permission to edit them.

*   [The Default Keybinding](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#the-default-keybinding)
    
*   [Changing the Keybinding](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#changing-the-keybinding)
    
*   [The Configs Screen](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#the-configs-screen)
    
*   [Server-Side vs. Client-Side](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/keybindings#server-side-vs-client-side)

# File Management - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

While the in-game GUI is convenient, sometimes you need to edit files manually (e.g., copying configs to a server via FTP).

### Location[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#location "Direct link to Location")

All configuration files generated by YAML Config are stored in the standard Minecraft config folder:

*   **Path:** `.minecraft/config/` (or `your_server_folder/config/`)

### Naming Convention[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#naming-convention "Direct link to Naming Convention")

Files are typically named using the following pattern: `[modid]-[filename].[extension]`

**Examples:**

*   `jobsplus-common.yaml`
*   `grieflogger-server.json5`
*   `necessities-client.toml`

### Editing Manually[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#editing-manually "Direct link to Editing Manually")

You can open these files with any text editor (Notepad, VS Code, Notepad++).

1.  **Stop the Server/Game:** It is safest to edit configs when the game is not running to avoid file conflicts.
2.  **Edit Values:** Follow the syntax of the file format (YAML, JSON, etc.).
3.  **Validation:** If you enter an invalid value (like text in a number field) and start the game:
    *   YAML Config will detect the error during loading.
    *   It will likely **reset that specific entry** to its default value to prevent the game from crashing.
    *   An error will be printed to the game logs.

### Server Syncing Explained[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#server-syncing-explained "Direct link to Server Syncing Explained")

This is a common point of confusion for server admins.

*   **Scenario:** You are playing on a server. You edit `jobsplus-common.yaml` in your local `.minecraft/config` folder using Notepad.
*   **Result:** Nothing happens in-game.
*   **Reason:** When you join a server, the server sends **its** config values to your client. Your local file is essentially ignored for gameplay logic while you are connected.
*   **Correct Way:** You must edit the file inside the **Server's** `config` folder and restart the server (or use the in-game GUI as an Operator to update it live).

*   [Location](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#location)
    
*   [Naming Convention](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#naming-convention)
    
*   [Editing Manually](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#editing-manually)
    
*   [Server Syncing Explained](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/file-management#server-syncing-explained)

---

# Using the Interface - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The YAML Config interface is designed to be intuitive while powerful enough to handle complex data structures. Here is a breakdown of how to interact with the various elements.

### Basic Elements[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#basic-elements "Direct link to Basic Elements")

#### 1\. Categories[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#1-categories "Direct link to 1. Categories")

Configuration files are often split into categories (e.g., "General", "Gameplay", "Rendering").

*   Clicking a category name expands or collapses it.
*   Nested categories are indented to show hierarchy.

#### 2\. Reset Button[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#2-reset-button "Direct link to 2. Reset Button")

To the right of every single setting, you will see a small **Reload/Reset Icon** 🔄.

*   **Grayed out:** The setting is currently at its default value.
*   **Active (White/Highlighted):** The setting has been changed from the default.
*   **Action:** Clicking this button instantly reverts that specific setting to the mod author's default value.

### Editing Values[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#editing-values "Direct link to Editing Values")

#### Booleans (True/False)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#booleans-truefalse "Direct link to Booleans (True/False)")

represented by a **Cycle Button**.

*   Click to toggle between `True` and `False` (or `On` and `Off`).

#### Numbers (Integers, Floats)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#numbers-integers-floats "Direct link to Numbers (Integers, Floats)")

Represented by a **Text Input Box**.

*   **Validation:** If you type a letter in a number field, the box will outline in **Red**, indicating an error.
*   **Ranges:** If the mod author set a minimum (e.g., 0) or maximum (e.g., 100) and you type `150`, the box will turn red. You cannot save until this is fixed.

#### Strings (Text)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#strings-text "Direct link to Strings (Text)")

Represented by a **Text Input Box**.

*   Some strings enforce patterns (like hex colors `#FF0000`). If the pattern doesn't match, the box turns red.

#### Enums (Dropdowns/Cycles)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#enums-dropdownscycles "Direct link to Enums (Dropdowns/Cycles)")

If a setting has a fixed list of options (e.g., `Difficulty: EASY, NORMAL, HARD`), it will appear as a **Cycle Button**. Click to cycle through the valid options.

### Advanced Collections[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#advanced-collections "Direct link to Advanced Collections")

YAML Config is unique in its ability to easily edit Lists and Maps in-game.

#### Lists[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#lists "Direct link to Lists")

A list entry (e.g., "Whitelisted Users") appears as a group.

*   **Add Entry:** Click the long **"Add Entry"** button at the bottom of the list to create a new row.
*   **Remove Entry:** Click the small **"X"** button next to a specific row to delete it.
*   **Reordering:** Currently, lists are ordered by insertion.

#### Maps (Key-Value Pairs)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#maps-key-value-pairs "Direct link to Maps (Key-Value Pairs)")

Maps appear as two text boxes side-by-side.

*   **Left Box:** The Key (e.g., Item Name).
*   **Right Box:** The Value (e.g., Price).
*   **Duplicate Keys:** If you type a Key that already exists in the map, the box will turn red to warn you of a conflict.

### Tooltips[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#tooltips "Direct link to Tooltips")

Hover over any setting name to see a **Tooltip**.

*   **Description:** Explains what the setting does.
*   **Default Value:** Shows what the original value was.
*   **Constraints:** Shows Min/Max values or required Regex patterns.

### Saving Changes[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#saving-changes "Direct link to Saving Changes")

At the bottom of the screen, you will see:

1.  **Cancel:** Discards all changes made in the current session and closes the menu.
    
2.  **Save Changes:**
    
    *   Writes the changes to the file on the hard drive.
    *   If applicable, sends the changes to the server.
    *   Fires an update event so the mod can apply changes immediately without a restart.
    
    _Note: If any field on the screen has a red validation error, the Save button will be disabled._
    

*   [Basic Elements](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#basic-elements)
    *   [1\. Categories](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/getting-started/using-the-gui#1-categories)

# Dependency Setup - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

To use YAML Config, you must add it to your development environment. Note that **YAML Config** depends on **UI Lib**, so both must be present.

### Gradle Properties[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#gradle-properties "Direct link to Gradle Properties")

Define the versions in your `gradle.properties` to manage updates easily.

    # Check the project page for the latest versionsyaml_config_version=19.0.1ui_lib_version=19.0.0

### Build Script (`build.gradle`)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#build-script-buildgradle "Direct link to build-script-buildgradle")

Add the Maven repository and the dependencies.

    repositories {    // DAQEM Studios Maven    maven { url "https://maven.daqem.com/" }}dependencies {    // 1. UI Library (Required Dependency)    modImplementation "com.daqem:uilib:${ui_lib_version}"    // 2. YAML Config    modImplementation "com.daqem:yamlconfig:${yaml_config_version}"}

### Fabric `fabric.mod.json`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#fabric-fabricmodjson "Direct link to fabric-fabricmodjson")

If you are developing for Fabric, add the dependency to your `fabric.mod.json` to ensure the game doesn't crash if the library is missing.

    "depends": {    "yamlconfig": ">=19.0.0",    "uilib": ">=19.0.0"}

### NeoForge `neoforge.mods.toml`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#neoforge-neoforgemodstoml "Direct link to neoforge-neoforgemodstoml")

For NeoForge, add the dependency block:

    [[dependencies.your_mod_id]]    modId = "yamlconfig"    type = "required"    versionRange = "[19.0.0,)"    ordering = "NONE"    side = "BOTH"[[dependencies.your_mod_id]]    modId = "uilib"    type = "required"    versionRange = "[19.0.0,)"    ordering = "NONE"    side = "CLIENT"\
\
*   [Gradle Properties](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#gradle-properties)\
    \
*   [Build Script (`build.gradle`)](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#build-script-buildgradle)\
    \
*   [Fabric `fabric.mod.json`](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#fabric-fabricmodjson)\
    \
*   [NeoForge `neoforge.mods.toml`](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup#neoforge-neoforgemodstoml)

# Validation & Comments - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

A good configuration file documents itself. YAML Config allows you to add extensive comments and metadata to your entries.

### Adding Comments[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#adding-comments "Direct link to Adding Comments")

Comments are added using the `.withComments(...)` method on any entry object.

    builder.defineBoolean("enable_features", true)       .withComments(           "Master switch for all features.",           "If false, the mod does nothing."       );

**Result (YAML):**

    # Master switch for all features.# If false, the mod does nothing.# Default value: trueenable_features: true

### Controlling Metadata[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#controlling-metadata "Direct link to Controlling Metadata")

By default, YAML Config appends the **Default Value** and **Validation Constraints** (like Min/Max) to the comments automatically. You can toggle this.

    builder.defineInteger("secret_value", 123)       .withComments(           false, // showDefaultValues           false, // showValidationParameters           "This is a secret."       );

### In-Game Tooltips[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#in-game-tooltips "Direct link to In-Game Tooltips")

The comments you define here are **automatically** converted into tooltips in the Configuration GUI. When a user hovers over the text of an entry, they will see your comments, making it easy to understand what the setting does without leaving the game.

### Validation Logic[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#validation-logic "Direct link to Validation Logic")

Every `IConfigEntry` has a `validate(T value)` method.

*   **Primitive Entries:** Check min/max bounds.
*   **String Entries:** Check length and Regex.
*   **Registry Entries:** Check registry existence.

If `validate` throws a `ConfigEntryValidationException`:

1.  The error is logged.
2.  The value is rejected.
3.  The entry reverts to its previous valid state or default.

*   [Adding Comments](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#adding-comments)
    
*   [Controlling Metadata](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#controlling-metadata)
    
*   [In-Game Tooltips](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#in-game-tooltips)
    
*   [Validation Logic](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation#validation-logic)

---

# The Config Builder - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `ConfigBuilder` is the entry point for creating configurations. It handles the definition of keys, default values, file generation, and registration with the internal `ConfigManager`.

### Initialization Lifecycle[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#initialization-lifecycle "Direct link to Initialization Lifecycle")

You should build your config during your mod's **initialization phase** (e.g., `onInitialize` in Fabric or the constructor/`FMLCommonSetupEvent` in NeoForge).

When `builder.build()` is called:

1.  The file is loaded from the disk (if it exists).
2.  If keys are missing, defaults are populated.
3.  The file is saved back to disk (ensuring new keys are written).
4.  The config is registered to the `ConfigManager` for the in-game GUI.

### Code Example[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#code-example "Direct link to Code Example")

    package com.example.mymod.config;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.impl.config.ConfigBuilder;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigType;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigExtension;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.entry.IBooleanConfigEntry;public class MyModConfig {    // Hold static references to your entries to access them globally    public static IBooleanConfigEntry IS_ENABLED;    public static void init() {        // 1. Create the builder        ConfigBuilder builder = new ConfigBuilder(            "my_mod_id",       // Your Mod ID            "general-config",  // File name (without extension)            ConfigExtension.YAML,            ConfigType.COMMON        );        // 2. Define Entries        IS_ENABLED = builder.defineBoolean("enabled", true)            .withComments("Set to false to disable all mod features.");        // 3. Category Stacking        builder.push("mechanics"); // Start 'mechanics' section            builder.push("movement"); // Start 'mechanics.movement'                builder.defineFloat("speed_modifier", 1.5f);            builder.pop(); // End 'movement'            builder.defineInteger("max_health", 20);        builder.pop(); // End 'mechanics'        // 4. Build and Load        builder.build();    }}

### The Stack System (`push` / `pop`)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#the-stack-system-push--pop "Direct link to the-stack-system-push--pop")

YAML Config uses a stack to determine the current nesting level.

*   **`push(String key)`**: Creates a new category (or "Map Node") under the current context and makes it the active context.
*   **`pop()`**: Returns to the parent context.

Important

Always ensure you have an equal number of `push` and `pop` calls. If you call `pop()` on the root level, an exception will be thrown.

### Accessing Values[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#accessing-values "Direct link to Accessing Values")

Once built, you can access values anywhere in your code using the entry reference:

    if (MyModConfig.IS_ENABLED.get()) {    // execute logic}

*   [Initialization Lifecycle](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#initialization-lifecycle)
    
*   [Code Example](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#code-example)
    
*   [The Stack System (`push` / `pop`)](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#the-stack-system-push--pop)
    
*   [Accessing Values](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder#accessing-values)

---

# JSON5 Format - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

**Extension:** `.json5` **Library:** [Jankson](https://github.com/falkreon/Jankson)

JSON5 is a superset of JSON that aims to make it easier for humans to write and maintain. It alleviates many of the strict limitations of standard JSON while remaining familiar to JavaScript and web developers.

### Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#features "Direct link to Features")

*   **Comments:** Supports both single-line (`//`) and multi-line (`/* ... */`) comments.
*   **Trailing Commas:** You can leave a comma at the end of a list or object without causing a parsing error.
*   **Unquoted Keys:** Object keys do not need quotes if they are valid identifiers (e.g., `{ key: "value" }` vs `{"key": "value"}`).
*   **Single Quotes:** Strings can use single quotes `'` or double quotes `"`.

### Implementation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#implementation "Direct link to Implementation")

To use JSON5, use `ConfigExtension.JSON5`.

    import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigExtension;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.impl.config.ConfigBuilder;ConfigBuilder builder = new ConfigBuilder(    "my_mod_id",    "client-settings",    ConfigExtension.JSON5 // <--- Selects .json5 format);

### Example Output[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#example-output "Direct link to Example Output")

    {    // HUD Rendering options    "hud": {        "enabled": true,        "position": {            "x": 10,            "y": 10, // Trailing comma is allowed!        }    },    /*    Network settings.    Don't touch unless you know what you are doing.    */    "network": {        "packet_limit": 50,        "ignored_packets": [            "packet_a",            "packet_b"        ]    }}

### When to use JSON5?[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#when-to-use-json5 "Direct link to When to use JSON5?")

Use JSON5 if you prefer the bracket-structure of JSON but need comments to explain your settings to users. It is robust and less error-prone regarding whitespace than YAML.

*   [Features](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#features)
    
*   [Implementation](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#implementation)
    
*   [Example Output](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#example-output)
    
*   [When to use JSON5?](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/json5#when-to-use-json5)
