# TOML Format - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

**Extension:** `.toml` **Library:** [NightConfig](https://github.com/TheElectronWill/Night-Config)

TOML (Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language) is widely used in the Minecraft modding ecosystem (particularly by NeoForge and Rust projects). It focuses on unambiguous semantics and is easy to parse.

### Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#features "Direct link to Features")

*   **Table-Based:** Uses `[headers]` to define sections (tables), making flat structures easy to read.
*   **Strong Typing:** clear distinction between integers, floats, booleans, strings, and datetime arrays.
*   **Comments:** Uses `#` for comments.
*   **Standard:** Highly standardized format with strict parsing rules.

### Implementation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#implementation "Direct link to Implementation")

To use TOML, use `ConfigExtension.TOML`.

    import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigExtension;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.impl.config.ConfigBuilder;ConfigBuilder builder = new ConfigBuilder(    "my_mod_id",    "common-config",    ConfigExtension.TOML // <--- Selects .toml format);

### Example Output[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#example-output "Direct link to Example Output")

    # General Mod Settings[general]# Is the mod enabled?enabled = trueversion = "1.0.0"# World Generation Settings[world_gen]generate_ores = true# Frequency of ore generation (0-100)ore_frequency = 15# Nested tables are represented with dots[world_gen.biomes]allow_desert = trueallow_forest = true# Lists look like standard arrays[items]starting_gear = ["minecraft:stone_sword", "minecraft:bread"]

### Considerations[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#considerations "Direct link to Considerations")

TOML is excellent for configurations that have many flat key-value pairs grouped into categories. However, deeply nested structures (e.g., a map inside a map inside a map) can become verbose due to the need for repeated headers like `[a.b.c.d]`.

*   [Features](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#features)
    
*   [Implementation](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#implementation)
    
*   [Example Output](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#example-output)
    
*   [Considerations](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/toml#considerations)

# Developer Guide - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Welcome to the **YAML Config** Developer Guide. This library is designed to abstract the complexities of file I/O, networking, and user interfaces, allowing you to implement robust configuration systems in minutes.

### Core Concepts[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#core-concepts "Direct link to Core Concepts")

1.  **The Builder Pattern:** You do not create config classes manually. You use the `ConfigBuilder` to define your structure programmatically.
2.  **Entry Objects:** When you define a config option, you get back an `IConfigEntry<T>` object. You keep a reference to this object to access the value later.
3.  **Automatic Networking:** If you use `ConfigType.COMMON`, the library handles synchronizing values from the logical server to connected clients automatically.
4.  **The Stack:** The configuration structure is hierarchical. You use a stack-based approach (`push`/`pop`) to create categories and sub-categories.

### Getting Started[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#getting-started "Direct link to Getting Started")

*   [**Dependency Setup**](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/setup)
     - Adding the library to your Gradle project.
*   [**The Config Builder**](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-builder)
     - Learning the syntax to create configs.
*   [**Config Types & Networking**](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types)
     - Understanding Client vs. Common vs. Server scopes.
*   [**Validation & Comments**](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/validation)
     - Adding constraints and user guidance.

### API Reference[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#api-reference "Direct link to API Reference")

*   [Primitives](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/entries/primitives)
     - Booleans, Integers, Floats, Doubles, Longs.
*   [Strings](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/entries/strings)
     - Text validation, Regex, and Enumerated Strings.
*   [Lists & Maps](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/entries/lists-maps)
     - Handling collections of data.
*   [Minecraft Types](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/entries/minecraft)
     - ResourceLocations and Registry entries.
*   [Special Types](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/entries/special)
     - Enums, DateTimes, and Custom implementations.

*   [Core Concepts](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#core-concepts)
    
*   [Getting Started](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#getting-started)
    
*   [API Reference](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer#api-reference)

# YAML Format - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

**Extension:** `.yaml` **Library:** [SnakeYAML Engine](https://bitbucket.org/snakeyaml/snakeyaml-engine)

YAML (YAML Ain't Markup Language) is the default and recommended format for **YAML Config**. It is designed to be human-readable and works excellently with hierarchical data structures, making it perfect for complex mod configurations.

### Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#features "Direct link to Features")

*   **Whitespace Sensitive:** Structure is defined by indentation (spaces), eliminating the need for curly braces or brackets.
*   **Native Comments:** Supports `#` comments which are preserved during save/load cycles.
*   **Clean Syntax:** Minimal punctuation makes it easy for non-developers to read and edit.
*   **Lists & Maps:** Natural syntax for defining lists (bullet points) and key-value maps.

### Implementation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#implementation "Direct link to Implementation")

To use YAML for your configuration file, pass `ConfigExtension.YAML` to your builder.

    import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigExtension;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.impl.config.ConfigBuilder;ConfigBuilder builder = new ConfigBuilder(    "my_mod_id",    "general-config",    ConfigExtension.YAML // <--- Selects .yaml format);

### Example Output[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#example-output "Direct link to Example Output")

Here is how a complex configuration looks in YAML:

    # General settings for the modgeneral:  # Enable debug mode to see verbose logging  debug_mode: false  # Game mechanic settings  mechanics:    # Player speed multiplier    # Default value: 1.0    speed_multiplier: 1.5    # List of allowed user groups    allowed_groups:      - "admin"      - "moderator"      - "vip"# Database connection settingsdatabase:  host: "localhost"  port: 3306  # Time (in seconds) before connection timeout  timeout: 30

### Best Practices[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#best-practices "Direct link to Best Practices")

*   **Indentation:** Always use **2 spaces** for indentation. Tabs are generally not supported in strict YAML.
*   **Strings:** Quotes are optional for simple strings, but recommended if your string contains special characters (like `:`, `{`, `[`, `#`).\
\
*   [Features](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#features)\
    \
*   [Implementation](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#implementation)\
    \
*   [Example Output](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#example-output)\
    \
*   [Best Practices](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/yaml#best-practices)

# Config Types & Networking - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The `ConfigType` enum determines where your config file lives, how it behaves in a multiplayer environment, and whether it automatically synchronizes.

### 1\. `ConfigType.CLIENT`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#1-configtypeclient "Direct link to 1-configtypeclient")

**File Location:** `config/` folder on the client.

**Behavior:**

*   Loaded only on the physical client.
*   Changes made in the GUI are saved immediately to disk.

**NO networking.** The server knows nothing about this config.

**Best For:** Rendering settings, GUI positioning, debug overlays, sound volumes.

### 2\. `ConfigType.COMMON`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#2-configtypecommon "Direct link to 2-configtypecommon")

**File Location:** `config/` folder on both Client and Server.

**Behavior:**

*   **Server Authority:** The server's file is the source of truth.
*   **Auto-Sync:** When a player joins, the server sends its values to the client via `ClientboundSyncConfigPacket`.
*   **Client Override:** While connected to a server, the client's local file values are ignored in memory (but the file on disk is not overwritten). The client uses the server's values.
*   **Editing:** If a player edits this in the GUI:
    *   If Singleplayer: Saves to disk and updates immediately.
    *   If Multiplayer (and Operator): Sends a `ServerboundSaveConfigPacket`. The server updates its file, and re-syncs all other players.
    *   If Multiplayer (not Operator): Editing is disabled.

**Best For:** Gameplay mechanics, recipe definitions, biome generation weights.

### 3\. `ConfigType.SERVER`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#3-configtypeserver "Direct link to 3-configtypeserver")

**File Location:** `config/` folder on the Server (or Singleplayer internal server).

**Behavior:**

*   Never sent to the client.
*   Can be edited via GUI by Operators, but the client doesn't hold a copy of the logic.

**Best For:** Database passwords, discord bot tokens, sensitive admin settings, server-side only logic (like chunk loading rates).

### Networking Implementation Details[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#networking-implementation-details "Direct link to Networking Implementation Details")

YAML Config handles the networking automatically.

1.  **On Player Join:** The `PlayerJoinEvent` triggers. It iterates over all registered `COMMON` configs and sends a synchronization packet to the joining player.

*   [1\. `ConfigType.CLIENT`](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#1-configtypeclient)
    
*   [2\. `ConfigType.COMMON`](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#2-configtypecommon)
    
*   [3\. `ConfigType.SERVER`](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#3-configtypeserver)
    
*   [Networking Implementation Details](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/developer/config-types#networking-implementation-details)

---

# HOCON Format - YAML Config | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

**Extension:** `.conf` **Library:** [Configurate](https://github.com/SpongePowered/Configurate)
 (wrapping TypeSafe Config)

HOCON (Human-Optimized Config Object Notation) is a superset of JSON developed by Lightbend. It is extremely powerful and is the standard for the Sponge ecosystem and many server-side plugins.

### Features[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#features "Direct link to Features")

*   **Less Noisy:** Commas, colons, and braces are often optional.
*   **Substitutions:** You can refer to other values within the config using `${path.to.value}` syntax.
*   **Includes:** Supports including other files, allowing you to split massive configs into multiple files.
*   **Duration/Size Support:** Natively understands values like `10m` (10 minutes) or `512MB` (though YAML Config maps these to Strings or Numbers for generic compatibility).

### Implementation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#implementation "Direct link to Implementation")

To use HOCON, use `ConfigExtension.HOCON`.

    import com.daqem.yamlconfig.api.config.ConfigExtension;import com.daqem.yamlconfig.impl.config.ConfigBuilder;ConfigBuilder builder = new ConfigBuilder(    "my_mod_id",    "advanced-config",    ConfigExtension.HOCON // <--- Selects .conf format);

### Example Output[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#example-output "Direct link to Example Output")

HOCON can look very similar to JSON, but cleaner:

    # Server propertiesserver {    # The port to listen on    port=8080    # Host address    host="0.0.0.0"}gameplay {    # Using substitution to reference the base difficulty    base-difficulty=10    # Calculated difficulty (conceptual example)    boss-difficulty=${gameplay.base-difficulty}0    features {        magic=true        tech=false    }}# Lists can be defined without commas if on newlineswhitelist=[    "Notch"    "Jeb"]

### When to use HOCON?[​](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#when-to-use-hocon "Direct link to When to use HOCON?")

HOCON is ideal for very advanced users or server administrators who might want to utilize variable substitution or file inclusion to manage complex modpacks.

*   [Features](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#features)
    
*   [Implementation](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#implementation)
    
*   [Example Output](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#example-output)
    
*   [When to use HOCON?](https://daqem.com/projects/yaml-config/wiki/formats/hocon#when-to-use-hocon)

Installing Tiny Mob Farm Remastered is straightforward, but it requires a few dependencies to function correctly.

Dependencies[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/installation#dependencies "Direct link to Dependencies")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You must install the following mods for Tiny Mob Farm to launch:

*   **[Architectury API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/architectury-api)
    ** - Required for cross-platform compatibility.
*   **[YAML Config](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/yaml-config)
    ** - Required for the configuration system.
*   **[UI Lib](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/ui)
    ** - Required for the in-game user interface.

Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/installation#steps "Direct link to Steps")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Download the `.jar` file for **Tiny Mob Farm Remastered** from [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/tiny-mob-farm-remastered)
     or [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/tiny-mob-farm-remastered)
    .
2.  Download the **dependencies** listed above for your specific mod loader.
3.  Place all files into your Minecraft instance's `/mods` folder.
4.  Launch the game!

# The Lasso - Tiny Mob Farm Remastered | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The Lasso is a magical item used to capture entities for processing inside a Mob Farm.

Crafting Recipe[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#crafting-recipe "Direct link to Crafting Recipe")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Usage[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#usage "Direct link to Usage")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Capturing[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#capturing "Direct link to Capturing")

**Right-click** any living entity with an empty Lasso to capture it. The entity's current health and attributes are saved into the Lasso.

### Releasing[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#releasing "Direct link to Releasing")

**Right-click** on a block while holding a full Lasso to release the mob back into the world.

Durability[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#durability "Direct link to Durability")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Lasso is **not** infinite. Every time a Mob Farm generates a drop, the Lasso takes durability damage.

*   **Base Durability:** 256
*   **Breaking:** If the durability reaches 0, the Lasso breaks, and you lose the captured mob.

tip

Different tiers of Mob Farms have different chances to damage the Lasso. Higher-tier farms are gentler on your Lassos!

Enchantments[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#enchantments "Direct link to Enchantments")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can enchant a Lasso using an Anvil or Enchanting Table:

1.  **Unbreaking:** Significantly increases the lifespan of the Lasso.
2.  **Looting:** Increases the amount of loot generated by the farm.

*   _Note: This must be enabled in the configuration file to work._

Restrictions[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#restrictions "Direct link to Restrictions")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Some mobs are too powerful to be captured. By default, the following mobs are blacklisted:

*   Ender Dragon
*   Wither
*   Warden

_(This list can be modified in the `tinymobfarm_common.yml` config file)._

*   [Crafting Recipe](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#crafting-recipe)
    
*   [Usage](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#usage)
    *   [Capturing](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#capturing)
        
    *   [Releasing](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#releasing)
        
*   [Durability](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#durability)
    
*   [Enchantments](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#enchantments)
    
*   [Restrictions](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/items/lasso#restrictions)

# Quick Start - Tiny Mob Farm Remastered | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Ready to start farming? Follow these simple steps to build your first farm.

Step 1: Craft a Lasso[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-1-craft-a-lasso "Direct link to Step 1: Craft a Lasso")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Lasso** is the core tool of the mod. It allows you to capture living entities.

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Step 2: Capture a Mob[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-2-capture-a-mob "Direct link to Step 2: Capture a Mob")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Find the mob you want to farm (e.g., a Cow, Chicken, or Zombie).

1.  Hold the **Lasso** in your main hand.
2.  **Right-Click** the mob.
3.  The mob will disappear, and your Lasso will now be enchanted and display the name of the captured mob.

Hostility

By default, the **Wooden** and **Stone** farms cannot process hostile mobs (Zombies, Skeletons, etc.). You will need at least an **Iron Farm** for those.

Step 3: Craft a Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-3-craft-a-farm "Direct link to Step 3: Craft a Farm")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Start with the **Wooden Mob Farm**.

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Step 4: Activate the Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-4-activate-the-farm "Direct link to Step 4: Activate the Farm")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Place the **Mob Farm** block down.
2.  **Right-Click** the farm to open the GUI.
3.  Place your **Lasso (with mob)** into the left slot.
4.  If the Lasso is compatible with the farm tier, the progress bar will start moving.

Step 5: Collect Loot[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-5-collect-loot "Direct link to Step 5: Collect Loot")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every time the progress bar fills up, loot is generated.

*   **Manual:** Open the GUI to take items out.
*   **Automatic:** Place a Chest or Hopper **directly underneath** the farm. The farm automatically ejects items downwards.

*   [Step 1: Craft a Lasso](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-1-craft-a-lasso)
    
*   [Step 2: Capture a Mob](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-2-capture-a-mob)
    
*   [Step 3: Craft a Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-3-craft-a-farm)
    
*   [Step 4: Activate the Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-4-activate-the-farm)
    
*   [Step 5: Collect Loot](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/getting-started/quick-start#step-5-collect-loot)

# Automation - Tiny Mob Farm Remastered | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Tiny Mob Farm Remastered is designed to be easily integrated into your storage systems.

Item Output[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#item-output "Direct link to Item Output")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mob Farm does **not** have an internal storage buffer for loot. It will attempt to eject items immediately upon generation.

*   **Direction:** Items are ejected **DOWN**.
*   **Storage:** Place a Chest, Barrel, Shulker Box, or Modded Storage block directly under the farm.
*   **Transportation:** Place a **Hopper**, Conveyor Belt, or Item Pipe directly under the farm to extract items.

Warning

If there is no container underneath the farm and no internal buffer space, items will be spawned into the world as entities. This can cause lag if left unattended!

Redstone Control[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#redstone-control "Direct link to Redstone Control")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can disable a farm using a Redstone Signal.

*   **Signal ON:** Farm pauses operation.
*   **Signal OFF:** Farm resumes operation.

This is useful for creating systems that automatically turn off the farm when your storage system is full to prevent item overflow lag.

Shift-Click Info[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#shift-click-info "Direct link to Shift-Click Info")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hovering over a Mob Farm item in your inventory while holding **SHIFT** will display detailed tooltips, including:

*   Exact generation rate.
*   Durability loss probability math.
*   Whether it supports hostile mobs.

*   [Item Output](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#item-output)
    
*   [Redstone Control](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#redstone-control)
    
*   [Shift-Click Info](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/automation#shift-click-info)

---

# Configuration - Tiny Mob Farm Remastered | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Tiny Mob Farm Remastered uses **YAML Config** for easy configuration. You can find the config file at: `config/tinymobfarm/tinymobfarm_common.yml`

General Settings[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#general-settings "Direct link to General Settings")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Lasso[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#lasso "Direct link to Lasso")

*   **`lassoDurability`** _(Default: 256)_
    *   Sets the maximum durability of the Lasso item.
*   **`allowLassoLooting`** _(Default: true)_
    *   If true, the **Looting** enchantment on a Lasso will increase drop rates.

### Blacklist[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#blacklist "Direct link to Blacklist")

*   **`blacklisted_mobs`**
    *   A list of Entity Resource Locations that cannot be captured.
    *   Default:
        *   `minecraft:ender_dragon`
        *   `minecraft:wither`
        *   `minecraft:warden`

Farm Settings[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#farm-settings "Direct link to Farm Settings")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For every tier (Wood, Stone, Iron, etc.), you can configure the following:

*   **`speed`**: The time in seconds it takes to generate one drop.
    *   _Example:_ `50.0` means one drop every 50 seconds.
*   **`enabled`**: If `false`, the farm cannot be crafted or used.
*   **`allows_hostile`**: If `true`, the farm can process hostile mobs (Zombies, Creepers, etc.).

### Example Snippet (Wood Farm)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#example-snippet-wood-farm "Direct link to Example Snippet (Wood Farm)")

    farms:  wood:    speed: 50.0    enabled: true    allows_hostile: false

Note on Updates

If you change the configuration on a server, you must restart the server for changes to take effect. Clients must also match the server's configuration for tooltips to be accurate.

*   [General Settings](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#general-settings)
    *   [Lasso](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#lasso)
        
    *   [Blacklist](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#blacklist)
        
*   [Farm Settings](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#farm-settings)
    *   [Example Snippet (Wood Farm)](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/configuration#example-snippet-wood-farm)

# Farm Tiers - Tiny Mob Farm Remastered | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Tiny Mob Farm Remastered features 8 different tiers of mob farms. Each tier improves upon the last by decreasing the operation time (speed) and reducing the durability damage taken by the Lasso.

Wooden Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#wooden-mob-farm "Direct link to Wooden Mob Farm")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The starting tier. Very slow and heavy on lasso durability. It cannot capture hostile mobs by default.

*   **Speed:** 50.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** High damage (2 or 3 durability per operation)
*   **Hostile Mobs:** No

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Stone Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#stone-mob-farm "Direct link to Stone Mob Farm")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Speed:** 40.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** Moderate damage (1, 2, or 3 durability)
*   **Hostile Mobs:** No

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Iron Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#iron-mob-farm "Direct link to Iron Mob Farm")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Iron tier is the first tier capable of processing hostile mobs.

*   **Speed:** 30.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** Low damage (1 or 2 durability)
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Gold Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#gold-mob-farm "Direct link to Gold Mob Farm")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Speed:** 20.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** Very low damage (1 or 2 durability, 2 is rare)
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Diamond Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#diamond-mob-farm "Direct link to Diamond Mob Farm")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Speed:** 10.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** Guaranteed 1 damage per operation
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Emerald Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#emerald-mob-farm "Direct link to Emerald Mob Farm")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Speed:** 5.0 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** Chance to take 1 damage, chance to take 0
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Inferno Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#inferno-mob-farm "Direct link to Inferno Mob Farm")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Speed:** 2.5 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** High chance to take 0 damage
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

Ultimate Mob Farm[​](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#ultimate-mob-farm "Direct link to Ultimate Mob Farm")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The pinnacle of mob farming. Lightning fast and completely preserves your Lasso.

*   **Speed:** 0.5 seconds per operation
*   **Lasso Durability:** 0 damage (Lasso never breaks)
*   **Hostile Mobs:** Yes

![Crafting Arrow](https://daqem.com/img/crafting/arrow.png)

*   [Wooden Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#wooden-mob-farm)
    
*   [Stone Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#stone-mob-farm)
    
*   [Iron Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#iron-mob-farm)
    
*   [Gold Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#gold-mob-farm)
    
*   [Diamond Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#diamond-mob-farm)
    
*   [Emerald Mob Farm](https://daqem.com/projects/tiny-mob-farm-remastered/wiki/blocks/farm-tiers#emerald-mob-farm)

# Configuration - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The X-Life mod features a straightforward configuration file that allows you to customize its core mechanics to better suit your server's needs.

### Locating the Config File[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#locating-the-config-file "Direct link to Locating the Config File")

After running the server with the mod installed at least once, a configuration file will be generated in the following location:

`<your-server-directory>/config/xlife.toml`

You can edit this file with any standard text editor. Remember to **restart your server** for any changes to take effect.

### Configuration Options[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options "Direct link to Configuration Options")

#### General Settings[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general-settings "Direct link to General Settings")

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `invert_hearts` | Boolean | `false` | If set to `true`, the heart color progression is inverted (e.g., 10 lives is red, 1 life is black). See the [Lives System](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system)<br> page for the full table. |
| `enchanted_golden_apple_adds_life` | Boolean | `false` | If set to `true`, players will gain one life upon eating an Enchanted Golden Apple. |

#### Debug Settings[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#debug-settings "Direct link to Debug Settings")

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `is_debug` | Boolean | `false` | Enables or disables debug mode. It is recommended to keep this `false` unless you are troubleshooting. |

*   [Locating the Config File](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#locating-the-config-file)
    
*   [Configuration Options](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#configuration-options)
    *   [General Settings](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general-settings)

# Lives System - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The Lives System is the core feature of the X-Life mod, introducing a high-stakes survival challenge where every death has a lasting consequence. Players begin with a set number of lives, and losing them gradually weakens you until you face your final death.

### How It Works[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system#how-it-works "Direct link to How It Works")

*   **Starting Lives:** By default, every player starts with 10 lives.
*   **Losing Lives:** Each time you die, you lose one life.
*   **Health Penalty:** Your maximum health is directly tied to your current number of lives. For every life you lose, your maximum health decreases by 2 points (one full heart).
*   **Final Death:** If you die when you only have one life left, it's game over. You will be permanently banned from the server with the message "You are out of lives."

### Gaining Lives[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system#gaining-lives "Direct link to Gaining Lives")

You are not doomed to permanently lose health. There are a couple of ways to regain lost lives:

1.  **Extra Life Item:** Using the [**Extra Life**](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life)
     item will restore one life, up to the maximum of 10.
2.  **Enchanted Golden Apple:** If enabled in the [**configuration**](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/configuration)
    , eating an Enchanted Golden Apple will grant you one additional life.

### Heart Colors[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/lives-system#heart-colors "Direct link to Heart Colors")

Your current number of lives is visually represented by the color of your health bar. This allows you and other players to quickly see how many lives you have remaining. The color progression can be inverted in the mod's configuration.

| Lives | Default Color | Inverted Color (`invert_hearts: true`) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **10** | Red | Black |
| **9** | Blue | Lime |
| **8** | Green | Cyan |
| **7** | Orange | Yellow |
| **6** | Pink | Purple |
| **5** | Purple | Pink |
| **4** | Yellow | Orange |
| **3** | Cyan | Green |
| **2** | Lime | Blue |
| **1** | Black | Red |

# Installation - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Installing the X-Life mod is a straightforward process. It is a server-side mod, but can also be installed on the client for single-player worlds. Clients do not need the mod to join a server running it.

Requirements[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before installing, please ensure you have the following:

*   [Architectury API](https://modrinth.com/mod/architectury-api)
    
*   [SuperMartijn642's Config Lib](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/supermartijn642s-config-lib)
    

Install All Dependencies!

The mod will not launch without **all** of its required dependencies. Make sure you download and install the correct version of each one for your chosen mod loader.

Installation Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps "Direct link to Installation Steps")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Download the Correct Files:** Download the X-Life mod and its dependencies for your specific mod loader (Fabric or Forge) and Minecraft version.

*   **X-Life Mod:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/x-life-health-mod)
    , [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/oWDu46gQ)
    
*   **Architectury API:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/architectury-api)
    , [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/architectury-api)
    
*   **SuperMartijn642's Config Lib:** [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/supermartijn642s-config-lib)
    

2.  **Install the Mod Loader:** Ensure you have the latest version of Fabric or Forge installed for your server or client.
    
3.  **Place Files in `mods` Folder:** Move the downloaded `.jar` files for the X-Life mod and both of its dependencies into your `mods` folder. If this folder doesn't exist in your server or client directory, create it.
    
4.  **Start Your Server or Client:** Start your game or server. The X-Life mod will generate its configuration file (`xlife.toml`) in the `config` folder on the first run.
    

*   Fabric
*   Forge

**Your `mods` folder should look like this for a Fabric setup:**

    /mods├── architectury-9.1.12-fabric.jar├── supermartijn642configlib-1.1.8-fabric-mc1.20.jar└── xlife-1.2-fabric-1.20.1.jar

_(Note: File versions may vary slightly)_

**Your `mods` folder should look like this for a Forge setup:**

    /mods├── architectury-9.1.12-forge.jar├── supermartijn642configlib-1.1.8-forge-mc1.20.jar└── xlife-1.2-forge-1.20.1.jar

_(Note: File versions may vary slightly)_

*   [Requirements](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements)
    
*   [Installation Steps](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps)

---

# Configuration - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Necessities generates two configuration files in your `config/necessities/` folder.

1\. `necessities.yml`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#1-necessitiesyml "Direct link to 1-necessitiesyml")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This file controls the logic and behavior of the mod.

### General[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general "Direct link to General")

*   **prefix**: The text displayed before system messages (e.g., `[Necessities]`).
*   **primaryColor**: The hex color code for the prefix brackets and main text.
*   **maxNickLength**: The maximum character length for `/nick`.
*   **allowColorsInNick**: Whether players can use color codes (e.g., `&6`) in their nicknames.
*   **godModeAllow**: Globally enable/disable the `/god` command.
*   **flyAllow**: Globally enable/disable the `/fly` command.

### Teleportation[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#teleportation "Direct link to Teleportation")

Control cooldowns (how long you must wait between uses) and delays (how long you must stand still before teleporting).

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `spawnTeleportDelay` / `spawnCooldown` | Settings for `/spawn`. |
| `warpTeleportDelay` / `warpCooldown` | Settings for `/warp`. |
| `homeTeleportDelay` / `homeCooldown` | Settings for `/home`. |
| `tpaTeleportDelay` / `tpaCooldown` | Settings for `/tpa`. |
| `backTeleportDelay` / `backCooldown` | Settings for `/back`. |
| `rtpCooldown` / `rtpDelay` | Settings for `/rtp`. |

### RTP (Random Teleport)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#rtp-random-teleport "Direct link to RTP (Random Teleport)")

*   **minRadius**: The minimum distance from spawn to teleport.
*   **maxRadius**: The maximum distance from spawn to teleport.

### Homes[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#homes "Direct link to Homes")

*   **homesLimit**: The default maximum number of homes a player can set. This can be overridden with permissions (see [Homes System](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes)
    ).

2\. `commands.yml`[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#2-commandsyml "Direct link to 2-commandsyml")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This file allows you to enable or disable specific commands and define custom aliases.

**Example Structure:**

    gamemode:  # Whether the command is registered  enabled: true  # List of alternate commands that trigger this  aliases:    - "gm"

If you want to disable `/tpa` entirely or change `/gmc` to run `/gamemode creative`, you can do it here.

*   [1\. `necessities.yml`](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#1-necessitiesyml)
    *   [General](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#general)
        
    *   [Teleportation](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#teleportation)
        
    *   [RTP (Random Teleport)](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#rtp-random-teleport)
        
    *   [Homes](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#homes)
        
*   [2\. `commands.yml`](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/configuration#2-commandsyml)

# Installation - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Necessities is a server-side mod. While it can be installed on the client for single-player use, its primary function is for multiplayer servers.

Requirements[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To run Necessities, you must have the following installed:

1.  **[Architectury API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/architectury-api)
    **
2.  **[YAML Config](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/yaml-config)
    **
3.  **[Fabric API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/fabric-api)
    ** (Fabric Users Only)

Installation Steps[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps "Direct link to Installation Steps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Download the `.jar` file for **Necessities** from [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/necessities)
     or [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/necessities)
    .
2.  Download the matching versions of **Architectury API** and **YAML Config**.
3.  Place all files into your server's `mods` folder.
4.  Restart the server.

*   Fabric
*   NeoForge

**Structure:**

    /mods├── architectury-x.x.x-fabric.jar├── fabric-api-x.x.x.jar├── necessities-x.x.x-fabric.jar└── yaml-config-x.x.x-fabric.jar

**Structure:**

    /mods├── architectury-x.x.x-neoforge.jar├── necessities-x.x.x-neoforge.jar└── yaml-config-x.x.x-neoforge.jar

Permissions Setup[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#permissions-setup "Direct link to Permissions Setup")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Necessities uses the standard permissions API for each platform.

*   **Fabric:** It is highly recommended to install a permissions manager mod like **LuckPerms**. If no permissions mod is installed, commands will default to Vanilla OP levels (Level 2 for most commands, Level 4 for admin commands).
*   **NeoForge:** Uses the internal NeoForge permission system. A manager like **LuckPerms** is also recommended here to utilize specific permission nodes.

*   [Requirements](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#requirements)
    
*   [Installation Steps](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#installation-steps)
    
*   [Permissions Setup](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/getting-started/installation#permissions-setup)

---

# Teleportation Commands - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

These commands handle movement across the world.

Spawn[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#spawn "Direct link to Spawn")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Teleports you to the world spawn point.

*   **Usage:** `/spawn`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.spawn`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

Set Spawn[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#set-spawn "Direct link to Set Spawn")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sets the world spawn point to your current location.

*   **Usage:** `/setspawn`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.setspawn`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

RTP (Random Teleport)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#rtp-random-teleport "Direct link to RTP (Random Teleport)")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Teleports you to a random location in the world within the configured radius.

*   **Usage:** `/rtp`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.rtp`
*   **Bypass Cooldown:** `necessities.command.rtp.bypass_cooldown`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

Back[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#back "Direct link to Back")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns you to your last location. This location is saved when you teleport or when you die (if `allowBackOnDeath` is enabled).

*   **Usage:** `/back`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.back`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

TPA (Teleport Ask)[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-teleport-ask "Direct link to TPA (Teleport Ask)")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Request to teleport to another player.

*   **Usage:** `/tpa <player>`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.tpa`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

TPA Here[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-here "Direct link to TPA Here")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Request another player to teleport to you.

*   **Usage:** `/tpahere <player>`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.tpahere`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

TPA Responses[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-responses "Direct link to TPA Responses")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Manage incoming requests.

*   **/tpaccept**: Accept a pending request.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.tpaccept`
*   **/tpadeny**: Deny a pending request.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.tpadeny`
*   **/tpatoggle**: Toggle whether you can receive TPA requests.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.tpatoggle`

*   [Spawn](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#spawn)
    
*   [Set Spawn](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#set-spawn)
    
*   [RTP (Random Teleport)](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#rtp-random-teleport)
    
*   [Back](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#back)
    
*   [TPA (Teleport Ask)](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-teleport-ask)
    
*   [TPA Here](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-here)
    
*   [TPA Responses](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/teleportation#tpa-responses)

# Items - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

The X-Life mod adds a crucial item to help you on your journey.

### Extra Life[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#extra-life "Direct link to Extra Life")

The **Extra Life** is a consumable item that serves as the primary method for regaining lost lives.

#### Usage[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#usage "Direct link to Usage")

To use the Extra Life, hold it in your hand and right-click, just like you would with an Eye of Ender. This will consume the item and launch a heart particle effect that travels a short distance before activating.

#### Effects[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#effects "Direct link to Effects")

When the Extra Life's effect activates, one of two things will happen:

*   **If you have 9 or fewer lives:** You will successfully regain one life. Your maximum health will increase by 2 points (one heart), and your heart color will update accordingly.
*   **If you have 10 lives (the maximum):** The effect will fail, and an Extra Life item will be dropped on the ground for you to pick up again. You cannot exceed the 10-life limit.

This makes the Extra Life a valuable and reusable resource once a player has reached the maximum number of lives.

*   [Extra Life](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#extra-life)
    *   [Usage](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#usage)
        
    *   [Effects](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/items/extra-life#effects)

# Player Management - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

On this page

Commands for managing player states and abilities.

Gamemode[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#gamemode "Direct link to Gamemode")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Change your gamemode.

*   **Usage:** `/gamemode <survival|creative|adventure|spectator> [player]`
*   **Alias:** `/gm` (Default)
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.gamemode`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

Fly[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#fly "Direct link to Fly")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Toggle flight mode.

*   **Usage:** `/fly [player]`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.fly`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

God Mode[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#god-mode "Direct link to God Mode")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Toggle invincibility. Prevents all incoming damage.

*   **Usage:** `/god [player]`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.god`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

Heal[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#heal "Direct link to Heal")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Restores health, saturation, eliminates fire, and removes negative potion effects.

*   **Usage:** `/heal [player]`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.heal`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

Feed[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#feed "Direct link to Feed")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Restores hunger and saturation levels.

*   **Usage:** `/feed [player]`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.feed`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

AFK[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#afk "Direct link to AFK")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Marks you as Away From Keyboard. This status is automatically removed if you move or send a chat message.

*   **Usage:** `/afk`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.afk`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

Nickname[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/player-management#nickname "Direct link to Nickname")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Change your display name in chat and the tab list.

*   **Usage:** `/nick <name>`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.nick`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

To remove a nickname:

*   **Usage:** `/delnick [player]`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.delnick` (Self), `necessities.command.delnick.others` (Others)

On this page

Ender Chest[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/inventory-utility#ender-chest "Direct link to Ender Chest")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Opens your ender chest (or another player's).

*   **Usage:** `/enderchest [player]`
*   **Alias:** `/ec`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.enderchest` (Self), `necessities.command.enderchest.others` (Others)
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

Inventory See[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/inventory-utility#inventory-see "Direct link to Inventory See")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

View and interact with another player's inventory in real-time.

*   **Usage:** `/invsee <player>`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.invsee`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

info

You can move items in and out of the target player's inventory.

Vanish[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/inventory-utility#vanish "Direct link to Vanish")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Become completely invisible to other players on the server.

*   **Usage:** `/vanish`
*   **Alias:** `/v`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.vanish`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

**Behavior:**

*   You are removed from the Tab list (for non-admins).
*   A fake "Left the game" message is broadcast when enabling.
*   A fake "Joined the game" message is broadcast when disabling.
*   You are invisible to entities and players.
*   Other players with permission `minecraft.command.gamemaster` (OPs) can still see you.

*   [Ender Chest](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/inventory-utility#ender-chest)

For general help, discussions, and real-time support, the best place to connect with the community and the developer is through Discord.

### When to Use Discord[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/support/discord#when-to-use-discord "Direct link to When to Use Discord")

*   **Questions & Help:** If you're having trouble with the configuration, don't understand how a feature works, or need general advice.
*   **Suggestions:** If you have an idea for a new feature and want to discuss it before creating a formal feature request on GitHub.
*   **General Chat:** Connecting with other server administrators and players who use the mod.

note

While you can ask for help with potential bugs on Discord, please be prepared to create a formal report on **[GitHub](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/support/reporting-issues)
** if the issue is confirmed to be a bug. The issue tracker is the official channel for tracking and fixing problems.

# Warps System - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/warps#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Warps are server-wide locations set by administrators that any player can teleport to.

Commands[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/warps#commands "Direct link to Commands")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **/setwarp \[name\]**: Sets a warp at your current location.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.setwarp`
*   **/warp \[name\]**: Teleports you to the specified warp.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.warp`
*   **/delwarp \[name\]**: Deletes a warp.
    *   Permission: `necessities.command.delwarp`

Management[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/warps#management "Direct link to Management")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warps are stored in the world data (level.dat) and are persistent across restarts. Unlike homes, warps do not have a hard limit on quantity, though listing them in chat may become cluttered if too many are created.

*   [Commands](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/warps#commands)
    
*   [Management](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/warps#management)

On this page

The Homes system allows players to save locations and teleport back to them later.

Commands[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#commands "Direct link to Commands")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **/sethome \[name\]**: Saves your current location. If no name is provided, it defaults to "home".
*   **/home \[name\]**: Teleports you to the saved home.
*   **/delhome \[name\]**: Deletes a saved home.

Home Limits[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#home-limits "Direct link to Home Limits")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, the number of homes a player can set is defined in `necessities.yml` under `homesLimit`.

However, you can give specific players or ranks different limits using permissions.

### Permission Nodes[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#permission-nodes "Direct link to Permission Nodes")

The mod checks permissions in a specific order:

1.  **Unlimited:** `necessities.home.limit.unlimited`
2.  **Specific Count:** `necessities.home.limit.<number>`

You can assign permissions like `necessities.home.limit.10` to VIPs to allow them to set 10 homes, while default players are restricted to the config limit. It supports limits up to 50 via permissions.

*   [Commands](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#commands)
    
*   [Home Limits](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#home-limits)
    *   [Permission Nodes](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/homes#permission-nodes)

# Commands - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

Learn More

On this page

The X-Life mod includes a simple but powerful command for server administrators to manage player lives.

info

All X-Life commands require a permission level of 2 or higher (typically reserved for server operators).

### `/xlife` Command[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/commands#xlife-command "Direct link to xlife-command")

This is the base command for all X-Life functions.

#### Set Player Lives[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/commands#set-player-lives "Direct link to Set Player Lives")

This command allows you to manually set a player's current life count.

**Syntax**

    /xlife set lives <target_player> <lives>

**Arguments**

*   `<target_player>`: The in-game name of the player whose lives you want to set.
*   `<lives>`: An integer between 1 and 10 representing the new life count.

**Functionality** Upon execution, the target player's lives will be set to the specified amount. Their maximum health and heart color will update instantly to reflect the change. A success message will be displayed in chat.

**Example** To set player Steve's lives to 5, you would use:

    /xlife set lives Steve 5

*   [`/xlife` Command](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/commands#xlife-command)
    *   [Set Player Lives](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/commands#set-player-lives)

# Chat - Necessities | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

Broadcast[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#broadcast "Direct link to Broadcast")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sends a message to the entire server with a configured prefix.

*   **Usage:** `/broadcast <message>`
*   **Alias:** `/bc`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.broadcast`
*   **Default Level:** 2 (OP)

Reply[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#reply "Direct link to Reply")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Quickly replies to the last person who sent you a private message (via vanilla `/msg` or `/tell`).

*   **Usage:** `/reply <message>`
*   **Alias:** `/r`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.reply`
*   **Default Level:** 0 (Everyone)

    ### 6. Feature Deep Dives#### KitsCreate `docs/necessities/wiki/features/kits.mdx`.

* * *

title: Kits description: Learn how to create and manage custom kits in Necessities.[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#title-kitsdescription-learn-how-to-create-and-manage-custom-kits-in-necessities "Direct link to title: Kits
description: Learn how to create and manage custom kits in Necessities.")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Necessities features a fully data-driven Kit system. Kits are collections of items that players can claim using the `/kit` command.

Using Kits[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#using-kits "Direct link to Using Kits")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **List Kits:** `/kit`
*   **Claim Kit:** `/kit <name>`
*   **Permission:** `necessities.command.kit`
*   **Bypass Cooldown:** `necessities.command.kit.bypass_cooldown`

Creating Kits[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#creating-kits "Direct link to Creating Kits")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Kits are defined via JSON files. You can add them in two ways:

1.  **Global Config:** Inside `config/necessities/kits/`.
2.  **Datapacks:** Inside `data/<namespace>/necessities/kits/`.

### File Structure[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#file-structure "Direct link to File Structure")

Create a file named `kitname.json`. The filename becomes the kit name.

config/necessities/kits/starter.json

    {  "cooldown": 3600,  "items": [    {      "id": "minecraft:stone_sword",      "count": 1    },    {      "id": "minecraft:bread",      "count": 16    },    {      "id": "minecraft:leather_helmet",      "count": 1    }  ]}

### Parameters[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Key | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cooldown` | Long | The time in **seconds** the player must wait before claiming the kit again. Set to `0` for no cooldown. |
| `items` | List | A list of item objects to give to the player. |

### Item Object[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#item-object "Direct link to Item Object")

The item object follows the standard Minecraft format (similar to recipes or loot tables).

    {    "id": "minecraft:diamond_sword",    "count": 1,    "components": {        "minecraft:enchantments": {            "levels": {                "minecraft:sharpness": 5            }        }    }}

*   [Broadcast](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#broadcast)
    
*   [Reply](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#reply)
    
*   [title: Kits description: Learn how to create and manage custom kits in Necessities.](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#title-kitsdescription-learn-how-to-create-and-manage-custom-kits-in-necessities)
    
*   [Using Kits](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#using-kits)
    
*   [Creating Kits](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#creating-kits)
    *   [File Structure](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#file-structure)
        
    *   [Parameters](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#parameters)
        
    *   [Item Object](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/commands/chat#item-object)

# Reporting Issues - X-Life | DAQEM Studios

[Skip to main content](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/support/reporting-issues#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

**Get 25% OFF** on your first order with **BisectHosting** using code "DAQEM"!

Get Discount

Learn More

On this page

A good bug report is the first step to getting a bug fixed. By providing detailed and accurate information, you make it much easier for the developers to identify, reproduce, and solve the problem.

**Before you post, please [search the existing issues](https://github.com/DAQEM/X-LifeMod/issues)
 to see if someone has already reported the same problem.**

### How to Create a Bug Report[​](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/support/reporting-issues#how-to-create-a-bug-report "Direct link to How to Create a Bug Report")

All bug reports should be submitted to our **[GitHub Issue Tracker](https://github.com/DAQEM/X-LifeMod/issues)
**.

Follow these steps to create a high-quality report:

1.  **Create a New Issue:** Click the "New Issue" button on the issue tracker page.
    
2.  **Write a Clear Title:** Your title should be a brief, clear summary of the issue.
    

*   **Bad Title:** `Mod doesn't work`
*   **Good Title:** `[Crash] Server crashes when a player with 1 life is killed by lightning`

3.  **Provide Essential Information:** In the body of the issue, please include the following details. This is the most important part of your report.

*   **X-Life Version:** (e.g., `1.2`)
*   **Minecraft Version:** (e.g., `1.20.1`)
*   **Mod Loader:** Fabric or Forge, including its version number.
*   **Full Mod List:** A list of all other mods you have installed.
*   **Server Logs / Crash Report:** Copy the full crash report or the relevant section from your `logs/latest.log` file.

Use a Paste Service

For long logs or crash reports, please use a service like [Pastebin](https://pastebin.com/)
, [Gist](https://gist.github.com/)
, or [mclo.gs](https://mclo.gs/)
 and link to it in your issue. Do **not** paste huge walls of text directly into the issue description.

4.  **Describe the Issue:**

*   **What happened?** A clear description of the bug.
*   **What did you expect to happen?** What the correct behavior should have been.
*   **Steps to Reproduce:** Provide a precise, step-by-step list of actions to trigger the bug. This is critical for debugging.

A detailed report will lead to a much faster resolution. Thank you for helping improve X-Life!

*   [How to Create a Bug Report](https://daqem.com/projects/x-life/wiki/support/reporting-issues#how-to-create-a-bug-report)

---

Creating Kits[​](https://daqem.com/projects/necessities/wiki/features/kits#creating-kits "Direct link to Creating Kits")

1.  **Global Config:** Inside `config/necessities/kits/`.

config/necessities/kits/starter.json
